More details — 10 passed · 3 could not be checked (detector unavailable)
  • ✓SpecThe spec files themselves are well-formed
  • ?Spec → CodeEvery realized_by surface and verification target file actually exists detector unavailable 3 MFAs declared
  • ?Spec → CodeEach verification target file references the requirement id it claims to cover detector unavailable
  • ?Spec → CodeEach realized_by binding still matches the live MFA closure detector unavailable 3 MFAs declared
  • ✓Spec → TestsEvery requirement is named by at least one verification entry 23 requirements declared
  • ✓Spec → TestsEvery must requirement under tagged_tests has a matching @tag spec: 23 requirements declared
  • ✓Spec → TestsEvery binding closure is exercised by at least one tagged test 3 MFAs declared
  • ✓Tests → CoverageVerifications that ran exited successfully
  • ✓Code → TestsTagged tests actually exercise the subject they claim
  • ✓CoverageEvery requirement reaches its minimum coverage strength 23 LINKED
  • ✓Spec → DecisionsEvery ADR referenced by a subject resolves to an existing decision file 1 reference
  • ✓Decisions / governanceEvery spec edit honors append-only governance and decision references
  • ✓BranchFiles changed in this PR have matching spec or decision updates

Triangulation: every requirement should link to code (realized_by), be exercised by a tagged test, and reach a verification claim. Above is what was actually verified for this change set.

  • specled.spec_review 1 finding
  • specled.triangulation clean
All findings (1)

Other 1 finding 1 info

  • info detector_unavailable specled.spec_review This subject declares no `realized_by` bindings — the SPEC ↔ CODE leg cannot be verified. Add a `realized_by:` block under `spec-meta` or on individual requirements to enable realization checks.
ℹ How is requirement coverage computed?

Each requirement is verified by one or more spec-verification entries that name it in their covers: list. The verifier inspects each entry and reports the strongest evidence it could find for the requirement.

EXECUTED
A kind: command entry with execute: true ran and exited 0; or a kind: tagged_tests entry executed its matching test successfully. Requires mix spec.check --run-commands (or mix spec.review --run-commands).
LINKED
For a file-kind verification (source_file, test_file, guide_file, …) the target file exists and its content references the requirement id (typically a # covers: comment or a literal mention). For tagged_tests, a test in the suite carries @tag spec: "<requirement_id>".
CLAIMED
The verification entry names the requirement in its covers: list. A textual claim only — nothing else has been checked yet.
UNCOVERED
No verification entry covers this requirement at all. Add one to spec-verification.

Tightening the floor. Pass --min-strength linked (or executed) to mix spec.check to require every claim reach that tier or fail. Set verification_minimum_strength: linked in a subject's spec-meta to bake that floor into the spec itself.

Priority (must / should / may) is the requirement author's RFC-2119 intent. Stability (stable / evolving) communicates whether the wording is settled or still being tightened. Hover any chip on this page for a one-line definition.

Decisions changed (0)

No ADR files changed in this change set.

Affected subjects (2)

Renders a Git change set as a self-contained HTML PR review surface. Spec-first navigation with prose-headlined subject cards, per-subject tabs (Spec / Code / Coverage / Decisions), top triage panel for findings, and a misc panel for unmapped changes. Same artifact runs locally and in CI.

specled.spec_review .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md SPEC EDITED 1 info 0 bindings (no realized_by declared)
Changes in this PR: 6 added

Requirements (15)

  • NEW specled.spec_review.triangle_code_classification must evolving

    The HTML artifact's sync diagram and sync checklist shall classify findings using the triangle vocabulary documented in docs/concepts.md. Specifically, findings carrying realization-side codes (`branch_guard_realization_drift`, `branch_guard_dangling_binding`) shall flip the SPEC ↔ CODE leg; findings carrying coverage-claim codes (`branch_guard_untested_realization`, `requirement_without_test_tag`, `branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag`) shall flip the SPEC ↔ TESTS leg; findings carrying observed-coverage codes (`branch_guard_untethered_test`, `branch_guard_underspecified_realization`) shall flip the CODE ↔ TESTS leg; within-spec consistency findings (`overlap/duplicate_covers`, `overlap/must_stem_collision`) shall flip the "spec files are well-formed" checklist row; and `append_only/*` findings shall flip a dedicated "Decisions / governance" checklist row.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state must evolving

    The sync diagram shall render a fourth per-leg state — `:degraded` — distinct from `:ok`, `:fail`, and `:vacuous`, used when a leg carries a `detector_unavailable` finding rather than a positive or negative verification. A degraded leg shall render with a `?` glyph (not `✓`) and a non-green color, and the leg's tooltip shall enumerate the distinct `detector_unavailable` reasons (`debug_info_stripped`, `umbrella_unsupported`, `no_coverage_artifact`, etc.) attributed to that leg. When any leg is degraded the artifact shall surface a top-of-page banner advertising that the report is partial. A `:fail` finding on the same leg supersedes `:degraded`; `:degraded` supersedes `:vacuous` and `:ok`.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.decisions_governance_inline must evolving

    The Decisions Changed section of the HTML artifact shall render `append_only/*` findings inline next to the ADR they should have authorized (matched by ADR id when the finding's `entity_id` resolves to a changed ADR), and shall surface findings that name no resolvable ADR — including unauthorized requirement deletions, modal downgrades, scenario regressions, and decision deletions — in a dedicated "Governance violations" subsection of the same panel. Each rendered finding shall preserve the code-fenced `fix:` block emitted by `SpecLedEx.AppendOnly.analyze` so reviewers see the remediation contract verbatim.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure must evolving

    Each subject's Coverage tab shall render, per requirement, a one-line bind-closure summary of the form "Closure: N MFAs. Reached: M (by tests T1, T2). Unreached: K." — where N is the size of the requirement's realization closure (the same closure the implementation tier hashes), and Reached plus the test list are computed from the `.spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata` per-test artifact via `SpecLedEx.CoverageTriangulation`. When the per-test coverage artifact is missing the tab shall render a single "Coverage artifact unavailable" banner in place of the per-row summaries; when the compiler tracer manifest is missing the tab shall render a single "Binding closure unavailable" banner. Both degraded states piggyback the page-level `:degraded` leg state machinery rather than rendering empty closure rows that would be misread as the absence of test coverage.

  • NEW specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code must evolving

    When an affected subject declares no `realized_by` bindings — the subject-level meta `realized_by` is absent or empty AND no requirement on that subject declares its own `realized_by` — the view-model build shall synthesize a single `detector_unavailable` finding per such subject with reason `no_realized_by` and severity `info`, attribute it to that subject, and route it through the `detector_unavailable_by_leg` aggregation under the SPEC ↔ CODE leg so the sync diagram renders that leg as `:degraded` (per `specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state`) and the partial-report banner enumerates `no_realized_by` as a distinct reason. A subject that declares any non-empty tier in its subject-level or requirement-level `realized_by` shall not produce this synthetic finding.

Show 10 unchanged requirements
  • specled.spec_review.html_artifact must evolving

    mix spec.review shall produce a self-contained HTML file with all CSS and JavaScript embedded so the artifact renders correctly without any external network resources at view time.

  • specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation must evolving

    The HTML shall organize content by spec subject as the primary unit, with each affected subject rendered as a card whose headline is the subject's human-readable prose statement rather than its raw YAML or file path.

  • specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs must evolving

    Each subject card shall expose four tabs - Spec, Code, Coverage, and Decisions - that pivot from the subject's intent to its supporting artifacts. The Code tab shall organize changes by realized_by binding tier rather than by file path.

  • specled.spec_review.triage_panel must evolving

    The HTML shall render a top-of-page triage panel that summarizes findings (count, severity breakdown) and the affected subject count for the change set. The panel shall collapse to a single confirmation row when there are zero findings, so clean change sets feel clean.

  • specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges must evolving

    Findings affecting a subject shall additionally render as inline badges on that subject's card, in addition to appearing in the top-of-page triage panel.

  • specled.spec_review.misc_panel must evolving

    Files that change in the diff but do not map to any spec subject shall be surfaced in a clearly-labeled "outside the spec system" panel as a flat file diff, rather than excluded from the artifact.

  • specled.spec_review.same_artifact_local_and_ci must evolving

    mix spec.review shall produce the same HTML artifact locally and in CI, sourced from the same code path with no local-only or CI-only rendering modes.

  • specled.spec_review.diff_against_base must evolving

    mix spec.review shall accept an explicit --base ref and otherwise default to the same base detection used by mix spec.next (origin/main, main, master, HEAD).

  • specled.spec_review.read_only_viewer must evolving

    The HTML artifact shall be a read-only viewer in v1 with no acceptance state, no per-subject sign-off, and no reviewer-attributed approval recorded by the artifact itself.

  • specled.spec_review.gh_pages_distribution should evolving

    A documented GitHub Actions workflow shall be provided that runs mix spec.review on PR open and synchronize, deploys the rendered HTML to a per-PR path on a GitHub Pages branch, and posts or updates a PR comment containing the link.

Scenarios (7)

  • NEW specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_renders_with_question_mark specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state
    given
    • a change set that produces a `detector_unavailable` finding with reason `:no_coverage_artifact`
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the CODE ↔ TESTS evidence leg renders with a `?` glyph rather than `✓` or `✗`
    • the leg's tooltip names the `no_coverage_artifact` reason
    • a top-of-page banner advertises that the report is partial
Show 6 unchanged scenarios
  • specled.spec_review.clean_pr_collapses specled.spec_review.triage_panel
    given
    • a PR whose change set produces zero findings
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the top-of-page triage panel collapses to a single confirmation row
    • no severity counts are surfaced
  • specled.spec_review.finding_renders_top_and_inline specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
    given
    • a PR whose change set produces a triangulation finding affecting subject S
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the top-of-page triage panel lists the finding with its severity
    • subject S's card carries an inline badge for the finding
  • specled.spec_review.unmapped_change_in_misc_panel specled.spec_review.misc_panel
    given
    • a PR that changes a file with no spec subject mapping (e.g., a dependency bump)
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • the file appears in the "outside the spec system" misc panel
    • the misc panel renders the change as a flat file diff
    • the file is not silently excluded from the artifact
  • specled.spec_review.self_contained_html specled.spec_review.html_artifact
    given
    • a rendered spec.review HTML artifact
    when
    • the file is opened in a browser with no network connectivity
    then
    • all CSS, JavaScript, and structural content render correctly
    • the artifact does not request any external network resources
  • specled.spec_review.subject_card_prose_headline specled.spec_review.spec_first_navigation
    given
    • a PR that affects subject S whose statement field is "S protects credentials at the boundary"
    when
    • mix spec.review renders the HTML artifact
    then
    • subject S's card headline is the prose statement, not the YAML id or file path
    • the YAML id is available as supporting metadata, not the primary headline
  • specled.spec_review.code_tab_grouped_by_binding specled.spec_review.per_subject_tabs
    given
    • a PR whose code changes touch MFAs in both the api_boundary and implementation tiers of subject S
    when
    • the reviewer opens subject S's Code tab in the rendered HTML artifact
    then
    • the changes are grouped under their realized_by tier headers
    • file paths appear under each tier rather than as the primary organization

lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex
@@ -159,6 +159,98 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.CoverageTriangulation do
159159 execution_reach(subjects, per_test)
160160 end
161161
162+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
163+ @doc """
164+ Returns per-requirement bind-closure reach data, keyed by
165+ `{subject_id, requirement_id}`.
166+
167+ Each entry is a map with:
168+ * `:closure_mfa_count` — total MFAs in the requirement's realization
169+ closure (length of `closure_mfas`).
170+ * `:closure_file_count` — total distinct files in the closure.
171+ * `:reached_files` — sorted list of closure files that any test
172+ exercised.
173+ * `:unreached_files` — sorted list of closure files that no test
174+ exercised.
175+ * `:reaching_tests` — sorted list of test display names
176+ (`"<file> :: <name>"`) that exercised at least one closure file.
177+
178+ Pure: `coverage_records` is the same input contract as `findings/3`, but
179+ this function does not branch on `:no_coverage_artifact` — pass the
180+ sentinel and you'll get `:no_coverage_artifact` back so the caller can
181+ render a "coverage artifact unavailable" message that piggybacks the
182+ `:degraded` leg state machinery.
183+ """
184+ @spec per_requirement_reach(coverage_input(), closure_map()) ::
185+ :no_coverage_artifact
186+ | %{
187+ optional({subject_id(), requirement_id()}) => %{
188+ closure_mfa_count: non_neg_integer(),
189+ closure_file_count: non_neg_integer(),
190+ reached_files: [Path.t()],
191+ unreached_files: [Path.t()],
192+ reaching_tests: [String.t()]
193+ }
194+ }
195+ def per_requirement_reach(:no_coverage_artifact, _closure_map), do: :no_coverage_artifact
196+
197+ def per_requirement_reach(records, closure_map)
198+ when is_list(records) and is_map(closure_map) do
199+ subjects = Map.get(closure_map, :subjects, %{})
200+ per_test = group_records_by_test(records)
201+
202+ Enum.reduce(subjects, %{}, fn {subject_id, info}, acc ->
203+ reqs = Map.get(info, :requirements, [])
204+
205+ Enum.reduce(reqs, acc, fn req, inner ->
206+ closure_files = req |> Map.get(:closure_files, []) |> Enum.map(&normalize_path/1)
207+ closure_mfa_count = req |> Map.get(:closure_mfas, []) |> length()
208+ closure_set = MapSet.new(closure_files)
209+
210+ {reached_files, reaching_tests} =
211+ Enum.reduce(per_test, {MapSet.new(), MapSet.new()}, fn t, {files_acc, tests_acc} ->
212+ hit =
213+ t.files
214+ |> Enum.filter(&MapSet.member?(closure_set, &1))
215+
216+ case hit do
217+ [] ->
218+ {files_acc, tests_acc}
219+
220+ hits ->
221+ {
222+ Enum.reduce(hits, files_acc, &MapSet.put(&2, &1)),
223+ MapSet.put(tests_acc, format_test_display(t))
224+ }
225+ end
226+ end)
227+
228+ reached_sorted = reached_files |> MapSet.to_list() |> Enum.sort()
229+ reaching_sorted = reaching_tests |> MapSet.to_list() |> Enum.sort()
230+
231+ unreached_sorted =
232+ closure_files
233+ |> Enum.uniq()
234+ |> Enum.sort()
235+ |> Enum.reject(&MapSet.member?(reached_files, &1))
236+
237+ Map.put(inner, {subject_id, req.id}, %{
238+ closure_mfa_count: closure_mfa_count,
239+ closure_file_count: closure_set |> MapSet.size(),
240+ reached_files: reached_sorted,
241+ unreached_files: unreached_sorted,
242+ reaching_tests: reaching_sorted
243+ })
244+ end)
245+ end)
246+ end
247+
248+ defp format_test_display(%{test_file: file, test_name: name}) do
249+ file = if file in [nil, ""], do: "(unknown)", else: file
250+ name = if name in [nil, ""], do: "(anonymous)", else: name
251+ "#{file} :: #{name}"
252+ end
253+
162254 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
163255 # Grouping + indexes
164256 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@@ -36,10 +35,31 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
3635 |> MapSet.to_list()

3837
38+ # covers: specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code
39+ # An affected subject with zero `realized_by` bindings produces a
40+ # synthetic `detector_unavailable` finding (reason `:no_realized_by`)
41+ # so the SPEC ↔ CODE leg renders as :degraded rather than silently ✓.
42+ # The same triangle-leg rendering pipeline (build_detector_unavailable_by_leg)
43+ # picks the synthetic findings up alongside tier-emitted ones.
44+ no_realized_by_findings =
45+ build_no_realized_by_findings(affected_subject_ids, subjects_by_id)
46+
47+ raw_findings = verifier_report["findings"] || []
48+ findings = raw_findings ++ no_realized_by_findings
49+ findings_by_subject = group_findings_by_subject(findings)
50+
3951 all_diffs = FileDiff.for_files(root, analysis.base, analysis.changed_files)
4052
4153 claims_by_subject = group_claims_by_subject(verifier_report)
4254
55+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
56+ # Per-requirement bind-closure reach: total MFAs, which closure files
57+ # were reached, by which tests. Degrades to a status atom when the
58+ # tracer manifest or coverage artifact is missing — the renderer
59+ # surfaces that as a "coverage artifact unavailable" message.
60+ closure_reach_opts = Keyword.get(opts, :closure_reach_opts, [])
61+ closure_reach_by_subject = CoverageClosure.build(index, closure_reach_opts)
62+
4363 affected_subjects =
4464 Enum.flat_map(affected_subject_ids, fn id ->
4565 case Map.get(subjects_by_id, id) do
@@ -55,7 +75,8 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
5575 all_diffs,
5676 findings_by_subject,
5777 root,
58− Map.get(claims_by_subject, id, %{})
78+ Map.get(claims_by_subject, id, %{}),
79+ Map.get(closure_reach_by_subject, id, %{status: :ok, by_requirement: %{}})
5980 )
6081 ]
6182 end
@@ -85,7 +106,6 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
85106 }
86107
87108 decisions_changed = build_decisions_changed(analysis, index)
88− findings = verifier_report["findings"] || []
89109 adrs_by_id = build_adrs_by_id(index, root, analysis.base)
90110 triage = build_triage(findings, affected_subjects, unmapped_changes != [], adrs_by_id)
91111
@@ -195,7 +215,8 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
195215 all_diffs,
196216 findings_by_subject,
197217 root,
198− claims_by_req
218+ claims_by_req,
219+ closure_reach
199220 ) do
200221 meta = subject["meta"]
201222 statement = meta_field(meta, :summary, "")
@@ -245,6 +266,7 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
245266 spec_changes: spec_changes,
246267 findings: Map.get(findings_by_subject, id, []),
247268 claims_by_req: claims_by_req,
269+ closure_reach: closure_reach,
248270 changed_files: changed_files
249271 }
250272 end
@@ -348,6 +370,8 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
348370 |> Enum.group_by(& &1["code"])
349371 |> Map.new(fn {k, v} -> {k, length(v)} end)
350372
373+ detector_unavailable_by_leg = build_detector_unavailable_by_leg(findings)
374+
351375 %{
352376 findings_count: length(findings),
353377 by_severity: by_severity,
@@ -361,10 +385,118 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review do
361385 adr_ref_count: length(adr_refs),
362386 unresolved_adr_count: length(unresolved_adr_refs),
363387 strength_breakdown: strength_breakdown,
364− findings_by_code: findings_by_code
388+ findings_by_code: findings_by_code,
389+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: detector_unavailable_by_leg
365390 }
366391 end
367392
393+ # Aggregate `detector_unavailable` findings into the triangle leg they
394+ # belong to, keyed by reason. Realization-tier detectors
395+ # (`debug_info_stripped`, `umbrella_unsupported` from any realization
396+ # tier) live on SPEC ↔ CODE; coverage triangulation's
397+ # `no_coverage_artifact` lives on CODE ↔ TESTS (the evidence leg).
398+ # Reasons we don't recognize land on `:unknown` so they aren't silently
399+ # dropped — the renderer surfaces them under the closest leg.
400+ defp build_detector_unavailable_by_leg(findings) do
401+ findings
402+ |> Enum.filter(&(&1["code"] == "detector_unavailable"))
403+ |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn finding, acc ->
404+ reason = finding["reason"] || "unknown"
405+ leg = detector_unavailable_leg(reason)
406+
407+ Map.update(acc, leg, %{reason => 1}, fn by_reason ->
408+ Map.update(by_reason, reason, 1, &(&1 + 1))
409+ end)
410+ end)
411+ end
412+
413+ # Reasons emitted by realization tiers (expanded_behavior, use_tier,
414+ # typespecs, api_boundary, implementation) live on SPEC ↔ CODE — the
415+ # `realized_by` leg. The coverage triangulation's `no_coverage_artifact`
416+ # lives on CODE ↔ TESTS — the evidence leg. `no_realized_by` (synthesized
417+ # by `build_no_realized_by_findings/2`) is the absence of any binding at
418+ # all, which lives on SPEC ↔ CODE for the same reason as the tier-level
419+ # reasons: there is nothing the realization detectors can verify.
420+ defp detector_unavailable_leg("debug_info_stripped"), do: :spec_to_code
421+ defp detector_unavailable_leg("umbrella_unsupported"), do: :spec_to_code
422+ defp detector_unavailable_leg("no_realized_by"), do: :spec_to_code
423+ defp detector_unavailable_leg("no_coverage_artifact"), do: :tests_to_coverage
424+ defp detector_unavailable_leg(_), do: :spec_to_code
425+
426+ # covers: specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code
427+ # Synthesize one `detector_unavailable` finding (reason `no_realized_by`)
428+ # per affected subject whose `spec-meta.realized_by` declares no bindings.
429+ # These findings flow through the same plumbing as tier-emitted detector
430+ # unavailables: they group per-subject, contribute to the triage panel's
431+ # `detector_unavailable_by_leg` aggregation, and surface the SPEC ↔ CODE
432+ # leg as :degraded with a reviewer-visible reason.
433+ defp build_no_realized_by_findings(affected_subject_ids, subjects_by_id) do
434+ Enum.flat_map(affected_subject_ids, fn id ->
435+ case Map.get(subjects_by_id, id) do
436+ nil ->
437+ []
438+
439+ subject ->
440+ if subject_has_realized_by?(subject) do
441+ []
442+ else
443+ [no_realized_by_finding(id, subject_file(subject))]
444+ end
445+ end
446+ end)
447+ end
448+
449+ defp subject_has_realized_by?(subject) do
450+ meta = subject["meta"] || Map.get(subject, :meta) || %{}
451+ subject_rb = realized_by_map(meta)
452+
453+ if realized_by_has_binding?(subject_rb) do
454+ true
455+ else
456+ reqs = subject["requirements"] || Map.get(subject, :requirements, []) || []
457+
458+ Enum.any?(List.wrap(reqs), fn req ->
459+ req |> realized_by_map() |> realized_by_has_binding?()
460+ end)
461+ end
462+ end
463+
464+ defp realized_by_map(nil), do: %{}
465+
466+ defp realized_by_map(container) when is_map(container) do
467+ case Map.get(container, :realized_by) || Map.get(container, "realized_by") do
468+ %{} = rb -> rb
469+ _ -> %{}
470+ end
471+ end
472+
473+ defp realized_by_map(_), do: %{}
474+
475+ defp realized_by_has_binding?(rb) when is_map(rb) and map_size(rb) == 0, do: false
476+
477+ defp realized_by_has_binding?(rb) when is_map(rb) do
478+ rb
479+ |> Map.values()
480+ |> Enum.any?(fn v -> v |> List.wrap() |> Enum.any?(&(&1 not in [nil, ""])) end)
481+ end
482+
483+ defp realized_by_has_binding?(_), do: false
484+
485+ defp subject_file(subject), do: subject["file"] || Map.get(subject, :file)
486+
487+ defp no_realized_by_finding(subject_id, file) do
488+ base = %{
489+ "code" => "detector_unavailable",
490+ "severity" => "info",
491+ "reason" => "no_realized_by",
492+ "subject_id" => subject_id,
493+ "message" =>
494+ "This subject declares no `realized_by` bindings — the SPEC ↔ CODE leg cannot be verified. Add a `realized_by:` block under `spec-meta` or on individual requirements to enable realization checks."
495+ }
496+
497+ if is_binary(file), do: Map.put(base, "file", file), else: base
498+ end
499+
368500 defp req_id(req) when is_struct(req), do: to_string(Map.get(req, :id) || "")
369501 defp req_id(req) when is_map(req), do: to_string(Map.get(req, :id) || Map.get(req, "id") || "")
370502 defp req_id(_), do: ""
lib/specled_ex/review/coverage_closure.ex
@@ -0,0 +1,267 @@
1+defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.CoverageClosure do
2+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
3+ @moduledoc """
4+ Computes per-requirement bind-closure reach data for the spec.review HTML
5+ Coverage tab.
6+
7+ The pipeline mirrors `mix spec.triangle`:
8+
9+ 1. Load the tracer manifest (callee edges) — without it the closure
10+ walk cannot run and the Coverage tab degrades to a "binding closure
11+ unavailable" message.
12+ 2. Walk the implementation closure for each subject via
13+ `SpecLedEx.Realization.Closure.compute/2`.
14+ 3. Load `.spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata` via
15+ `SpecLedEx.Coverage.Store`. Missing artifact → degrade to a
16+ "coverage artifact unavailable" message that piggybacks the
17+ `:degraded` leg state machinery (see
18+ `specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state`).
19+ 4. Hand both into `SpecLedEx.CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach/2`
20+ which returns the per-requirement reach summary the renderer turns
21+ into "Closure: N MFAs. Reached: M (by tests T1, T2). Unreached: K."
22+
23+ The function is read-only: missing artifacts produce a status atom rather
24+ than raising, so spec.review keeps rendering even when triangulation
25+ inputs are absent (the same posture used by spec.triangle).
26+ """
27+
28+ alias SpecLedEx.Compiler.Tracer
29+ alias SpecLedEx.Coverage.Store
30+ alias SpecLedEx.CoverageTriangulation
31+ alias SpecLedEx.Realization.Closure
32+
33+ @type reach_status :: :ok | :no_coverage_artifact | :no_tracer_manifest
34+
35+ @type subject_reach :: %{
36+ status: reach_status(),
37+ by_requirement: %{optional(String.t()) => map()}
38+ }
39+
40+ @doc """
41+ Returns `%{subject_id => subject_reach}` for every subject in `index`.
42+
43+ Each `subject_reach` carries:
44+
45+ * `:status` — `:ok` when both the tracer manifest and coverage
46+ artifact were loaded, `:no_tracer_manifest` when the tracer manifest
47+ is missing (closure walk skipped), `:no_coverage_artifact` when the
48+ manifest loaded but `.spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata` is missing.
49+ * `:by_requirement` — `%{requirement_id => reach_map}` from
50+ `CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach/2`. Empty when status
51+ is degraded.
52+
53+ `opts` (all optional, primarily for tests):
54+
55+ * `:tracer_edges` — pre-loaded tracer-edge map (skips disk read).
56+ * `:coverage_records` — pre-loaded coverage records OR
57+ `:no_coverage_artifact` (skips disk read).
58+ * `:artifact_path` — override `.spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata`.
59+ """
60+ @spec build(map(), keyword()) :: %{optional(String.t()) => subject_reach()}
61+ def build(index, opts \\ []) when is_map(index) do
62+ tracer_edges =
63+ case Keyword.fetch(opts, :tracer_edges) do
64+ {:ok, edges} -> edges
65+ :error -> load_tracer_edges()
66+ end
67+
68+ coverage_records =
69+ case Keyword.fetch(opts, :coverage_records) do
70+ {:ok, records} ->
71+ records
72+
73+ :error ->
74+ path = Keyword.get(opts, :artifact_path) || Store.default_path()
75+ load_coverage(path)
76+ end
77+
78+ subjects = normalized_subjects(index)
79+
80+ cond do
81+ tracer_edges == %{} ->
82+ Map.new(subjects, fn s ->
83+ {s.id, %{status: :no_tracer_manifest, by_requirement: %{}}}
84+ end)
85+
86+ coverage_records == :no_coverage_artifact ->
87+ Map.new(subjects, fn s ->
88+ {s.id, %{status: :no_coverage_artifact, by_requirement: %{}}}
89+ end)
90+
91+ true ->
92+ world = %{subjects: subjects, tracer_edges: tracer_edges}
93+
94+ Map.new(subjects, fn subject ->
95+ closure = Closure.compute(subject, world)
96+
97+ requirements =
98+ requirements_for(index, subject.id)
99+ |> Enum.map(fn req -> requirement_view(req, closure) end)
100+
101+ closure_map = %{
102+ subjects: %{
103+ subject.id => %{
104+ owned_files: subject.surface,
105+ requirements: requirements
106+ }
107+ }
108+ }
109+
110+ per_req = CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach(coverage_records, closure_map)
111+
112+ by_req =
113+ case per_req do
114+ :no_coverage_artifact ->
115+ %{}
116+
117+ map when is_map(map) ->
118+ Map.new(requirements, fn req ->
119+ {req.id, Map.get(map, {subject.id, req.id}, empty_reach(req))}
120+ end)
121+ end
122+
123+ {subject.id, %{status: :ok, by_requirement: by_req}}
124+ end)
125+ end
126+ end
127+
128+ defp empty_reach(req) do
129+ %{
130+ closure_mfa_count: length(Map.get(req, :closure_mfas, [])),
131+ closure_file_count: req |> Map.get(:closure_files, []) |> Enum.uniq() |> length(),
132+ reached_files: [],
133+ unreached_files: req |> Map.get(:closure_files, []) |> Enum.uniq() |> Enum.sort(),
134+ reaching_tests: []
135+ }
136+ end
137+
138+ # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139+ # Closure plumbing — mirrors mix spec.triangle so behavior stays consistent.
140+ # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
141+
142+ defp load_tracer_edges do
143+ path = Tracer.manifest_path()
144+
145+ with true <- File.regular?(path),
146+ {:ok, binary} <- File.read(path),
147+ map when is_map(map) <- :erlang.binary_to_term(binary) do
148+ map
149+ else
150+ _ -> %{}
151+ end
152+ rescue
153+ _ -> %{}
154+ end
155+
156+ defp load_coverage(path) do
157+ if File.regular?(path) do
158+ Store.read(path)
159+ else
160+ :no_coverage_artifact
161+ end
162+ end
163+
164+ defp normalized_subjects(index) do
165+ index
166+ |> Map.get("subjects", [])
167+ |> Enum.map(fn subject ->
168+ meta = fetch_field(subject, "meta")
169+ id = fetch_field(meta, "id")
170+
171+ surface =
172+ meta
173+ |> fetch_field("surface")
174+ |> List.wrap()
175+ |> Enum.filter(&is_binary/1)
176+
177+ impl =
178+ meta
179+ |> fetch_field("realized_by")
180+ |> case do
181+ %{} = rb -> rb
182+ _ -> %{}
183+ end
184+ |> normalize_keys()
185+ |> Map.get("implementation", [])
186+ |> List.wrap()
187+
188+ %{id: id || "<unknown>", surface: surface, impl_bindings: impl}
189+ end)
190+ |> Enum.reject(&(&1.id == "<unknown>"))
191+ end
192+
193+ defp normalize_keys(map) when is_map(map) do
194+ Map.new(map, fn
195+ {k, v} when is_atom(k) -> {Atom.to_string(k), v}
196+ pair -> pair
197+ end)
198+ end
199+
200+ defp requirements_for(index, subject_id) do
201+ index
202+ |> Map.get("subjects", [])
203+ |> Enum.find(fn s ->
204+ meta = fetch_field(s, "meta")
205+ fetch_field(meta, "id") == subject_id
206+ end)
207+ |> case do
208+ nil -> []
209+ subject -> subject |> fetch_field("requirements") |> List.wrap() |> Enum.filter(&is_map/1)
210+ end
211+ end
212+
213+ defp requirement_view(req, closure) do
214+ req_id = fetch_field(req, "id")
215+
216+ closure_mfa_tuples =
217+ if closure do
218+ closure.owned_mfas ++ closure.shared_mfas
219+ else
220+ []
221+ end
222+
223+ closure_files =
224+ closure_mfa_tuples
225+ |> Enum.flat_map(&mfa_source_file/1)
226+ |> Enum.uniq()
227+
228+ %{
229+ id: req_id,
230+ binding_present?: closure_files != [],
231+ closure_files: closure_files,
232+ closure_mfas: Enum.map(closure_mfa_tuples, &mfa_to_string/1)
233+ }
234+ end
235+
236+ defp mfa_to_string({mod, fun, arity}), do: "#{inspect(mod)}.#{fun}/#{arity}"
237+ defp mfa_to_string(other), do: to_string(other)
238+
239+ defp mfa_source_file({mod, _fun, _arity}) do
240+ case Code.ensure_loaded(mod) do
241+ {:module, ^mod} ->
242+ case mod.module_info(:compile)[:source] do
243+ path when is_list(path) -> [List.to_string(path)]
244+ path when is_binary(path) -> [path]
245+ _ -> []
246+ end
247+
248+ _ ->
249+ []
250+ end
251+ rescue
252+ _ -> []
253+ end
254+
255+ defp fetch_field(map, key) when is_map(map) and is_binary(key) do
256+ atom_key =
257+ try do
258+ String.to_existing_atom(key)
259+ rescue
260+ ArgumentError -> nil
261+ end
262+
263+ Map.get(map, key, if(atom_key, do: Map.get(map, atom_key)))
264+ end
265+
266+ defp fetch_field(_, _), do: nil
267+end
lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
@@ -94,9 +94,11 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
9494 <main>
9595 <section class="view-pane view-pane-spec active" data-view-pane="spec">
9696 <%= render_triage(view.triage, view.all_findings) %>
97− <%= render_decisions_changed(view.decisions_changed, view.adrs_by_id) %>
97+ <%= render_coverage_help_disclosure() %>
98+ <%= render_decisions_changed(view.decisions_changed, view.adrs_by_id, view.all_findings) %>
9899 <%= render_subjects(view.affected_subjects, view.adrs_by_id) %>
99100 <%= render_unmapped(view.unmapped_changes, view.file_breakdown) %>
101+ <%= render_raw_verification_all(view.affected_subjects) %>
100102 </section>
101103 <section class="view-pane view-pane-files" data-view-pane="files">
102104 <%= render_files_view(view.all_changes, view.meta.stats) %>
@@ -260,6 +262,7 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
260262
261263 ~s"""
262264 <header class="sync-headline">
265+ #{render_degraded_banner(triage)}
263266 <div class="sync-headline-row">
264267 <span class="sync-headline-icon #{icon_class}" aria-hidden="true">#{icon}</span>
265268 <div class="sync-headline-text">
@@ -272,22 +275,99 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
272275 """
273276 end
274277
275− defp render_sync_diagram(triage, _in_sync?) do
278+ # covers: specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state
279+ # When any triangle leg is degraded by a `detector_unavailable` finding
280+ # the reviewer should see immediately that the report is partial — no
281+ # amount of green ✓s on adjacent legs makes a missing leg "fine."
282+ @doc false
283+ def render_degraded_banner(triage) do
284+ reasons =
285+ triage
286+ |> Map.get(:detector_unavailable_by_leg, %{})
287+ |> Map.values()
288+ |> Enum.flat_map(&Map.keys/1)
289+ |> Enum.uniq()
290+ |> Enum.sort()
291+
292+ case reasons do
293+ [] ->
294+ ""
295+
296+ reasons ->
297+ formatted =
298+ reasons
299+ |> Enum.map(&"<code>#{h(&1)}</code>")
300+ |> Enum.join(", ")
301+
302+ ~s|<div class="sync-degraded-banner" role="status"><span class="sync-degraded-icon" aria-hidden="true">?</span><span class="sync-degraded-text">Partial report — one or more triangle legs could not be checked on this run (#{formatted}). Re-run with the missing detector input to verify these legs.</span></div>|
303+ end
304+ end
305+
306+ # covers: specled.spec_review.triangle_code_classification
307+ # Triangle leg vocabulary mapped to the three sides of the spec/code/test
308+ # triangle described in docs/concepts.md. The diagram and the checklist
309+ # both feed off `findings_by_code`; together they advertise which side of
310+ # the triangle a finding lives on.
311+ @doc false
312+ def render_sync_diagram(triage, _in_sync? \\ false) do
276313 fbc = triage.findings_by_code
277314
315+ # Triangle leg vocabulary, per docs/concepts.md:
316+ # * SPEC ↔ CODE — realized_by binding integrity (surfaces exist,
317+ # hashes match, bindings point at live MFAs).
318+ # * SPEC ↔ TESTS — @tag spec claim integrity (every covered
319+ # requirement is named by a test, every binding closure is
320+ # exercised by some tagged test, and verification commands run).
321+ # * CODE ↔ TESTS — observed-coverage integrity (tests exercise the
322+ # code their tag claims, MFAs aren't silently executed without an
323+ # owning requirement, and strength minimums are met).
278324 spec_to_code_codes =
279− ~w(surface_target_missing verification_target_missing verification_target_missing_file verification_target_missing_reference)
325+ ~w(
326+ surface_target_missing
327+ verification_target_missing
328+ verification_target_missing_file
329+ verification_target_missing_reference
330+ branch_guard_realization_drift
331+ branch_guard_dangling_binding
332+ )
280333
281− code_to_tests_codes = ~w(verification_command_failed)
334+ code_to_tests_codes =
335+ ~w(
336+ verification_command_failed
337+ branch_guard_untested_realization
338+ requirement_without_test_tag
339+ branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag
340+ )
282341
283342 tests_to_coverage_codes =
284− ~w(verification_strength_below_minimum requirement_without_verification verification_unknown_cover)
343+ ~w(
344+ verification_strength_below_minimum
345+ requirement_without_verification
346+ verification_unknown_cover
347+ branch_guard_untethered_test
348+ branch_guard_underspecified_realization
349+ )
350+
351+ detector_by_leg = Map.get(triage, :detector_unavailable_by_leg, %{})
352+ spec_to_code_degraded = Map.get(detector_by_leg, :spec_to_code, %{})
353+ spec_to_tests_degraded = Map.get(detector_by_leg, :spec_to_tests, %{})
354+ tests_to_coverage_degraded = Map.get(detector_by_leg, :tests_to_coverage, %{})
285355
286356 # Vacuous: the leg has nothing to verify on this PR. Reads as gray, not
287357 # green — vacuous truth should not look like a victory.
288− spec_to_code_state = leg_state(fbc, spec_to_code_codes, triage.binding_count == 0)
289− code_to_tests_state = leg_state(fbc, code_to_tests_codes, triage.binding_count == 0)
290− tests_to_coverage_state = leg_state(fbc, tests_to_coverage_codes, triage.verification_count == 0)
358+ # Degraded: the leg has a `detector_unavailable` finding (debug_info
359+ # stripped, umbrella unsupported, missing coverage artifact, …). Reads
360+ # as gray-yellow with a `?` glyph — the system is being honest that it
361+ # could not run the check.
362+ spec_to_code_state =
363+ leg_state(fbc, spec_to_code_codes, triage.binding_count == 0, map_size(spec_to_code_degraded) > 0)
364+
365+ code_to_tests_state =
366+ leg_state(fbc, code_to_tests_codes, triage.binding_count == 0, map_size(spec_to_tests_degraded) > 0)
367+
368+ tests_to_coverage_state =
369+ leg_state(fbc, tests_to_coverage_codes, triage.verification_count == 0,
370+ map_size(tests_to_coverage_degraded) > 0)
291371
292372 nodes = [
293373 {"SPEC", "#{triage.affected_subject_count} subj · #{triage.requirement_count} req#{maybe_s(triage.requirement_count)}",

327410
328− # Tri-state per-leg evaluation. :vacuous means "nothing to verify on this
329− # leg" — we should not render that as ✓ since there is no positive
411+ # Quad-state per-leg evaluation. :vacuous means "nothing to verify on
412+ # this leg" — we should not render that as ✓ since there is no positive
330413 # evidence. :ok means "all relevant checks passed". :fail means "some
331− # finding hit one of this leg's codes".
332− defp leg_state(fbc, codes, vacuous?) do
414+ # finding hit one of this leg's codes". :degraded means "the detector
415+ # for this leg could not run (e.g. detector_unavailable: debug_info_
416+ # stripped, umbrella_unsupported, no_coverage_artifact)" — neither
417+ # positive nor negative evidence; the system is being honest that the
418+ # check did not run. :fail wins over :degraded; :degraded wins over
419+ # :vacuous and :ok.
420+ # covers: specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state
421+ defp leg_state(fbc, codes, vacuous?, degraded?) do
333422 cond do
334423 Enum.any?(codes, fn c -> Map.get(fbc, c, 0) > 0 end) -> :fail
424+ degraded? -> :degraded
335425 vacuous? -> :vacuous
336426 true -> :ok
337427 end
@@ -348,12 +438,29 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
348438 case state do
349439 :ok -> {"ok", "✓"}
350440 :fail -> {"fail", "✗"}
441+ :degraded -> {"degraded", "?"}
351442 :vacuous -> {"vacuous", "—"}
352443 end
353444
354445 ~s|<div class="sync-edge sync-edge-#{state_class}" title="#{html_escape(tooltip)}"><div class="sync-edge-line"></div><div class="sync-edge-label"><span class="sync-edge-icon">#{icon}</span> #{h(label)}</div></div>|
355446 end
356447
448+ # Append a "Degraded: <reason>, <reason>" suffix to an edge tooltip when
449+ # the leg carries detector_unavailable findings. Reasons are rendered
450+ # verbatim (snake_case) since they're a fixed catalog the reviewer can
451+ # look up in docs/concepts.md.
452+ defp degraded_tooltip_suffix(reasons_map) when reasons_map == %{}, do: ""
453+
454+ defp degraded_tooltip_suffix(reasons_map) when is_map(reasons_map) do
455+ reasons =
456+ reasons_map
457+ |> Map.keys()
458+ |> Enum.sort()
459+ |> Enum.join(", ")
460+
461+ " · Degraded: #{reasons}"
462+ end
463+
357464 defp strength_diagram_summary(breakdown) when is_map(breakdown) and map_size(breakdown) > 0 do
358465 [:executed, :linked, :claimed, :uncovered]
359466 |> Enum.map(fn k -> {k, Map.get(breakdown, k, 0)} end)

397− passed_count = length(checks) - failed_count - vacuous_count

504+

506+ Enum.count(checks, fn c ->
507+ c.passed? and Map.get(c, :vacuous?, false) and not Map.get(c, :degraded?, false)
508+ end)
509+
510+ passed_count = length(checks) - failed_count - vacuous_count - degraded_count
511+ open_attr = if failed_count > 0 or degraded_count > 0, do: " open", else: ""
399512

401514 cond do
402515 failed_count > 0 ->
403516 "More details — #{failed_count} of #{length(checks)} checks need attention"
404517

@@ -566,40 +776,243 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
566776 defp maybe_s(1), do: ""
567777 defp maybe_s(_), do: "s"
568778
569− defp render_findings_list([]), do: ""
779+ @doc false
780+ def render_findings_list([]), do: ""
781+
782+ # covers: specled.spec_review.triage_panel
783+ # Group findings by triangle leg, then by severity within each leg, so a
784+ # busy PR's flat list becomes scannable. The leg classifier reuses the
785+ # code-to-leg mapping that drives the sync checklist (build_sync_checks)
786+ # rather than maintaining a parallel table.
787+ def render_findings_list(findings) do
788+ grouped = group_findings_by_leg(findings)
789+ total = length(findings)
570790
571− defp render_findings_list(findings) do
572791 [
573− ~S"""
574− <details class="findings-list" open>
575− <summary>All findings</summary>
576− <ul>
577− """,
578− Enum.map(findings, fn f ->
579− sev = f["severity"] || ""
580−
581− ~s"""
582− <li class="finding-item finding-#{h(sev)}">
583− <span class="finding-severity"#{title_attr(tooltip(:severity, sev))}>#{h(sev)}</span>
584− <span class="finding-code">#{h(f["code"])}</span>
585− #{render_subject_link(f["subject_id"])}
586− <span class="finding-message">#{h(f["message"])}</span>
587− </li>
588− """
589− end),
590− ~S"""
591− </ul>
592− </details>
593− """
792+ ~s|<details class="findings-list" open>|,
793+ ~s|<summary>All findings (#{total})</summary>|,
794+ Enum.map(grouped, &render_findings_group/1),
795+ ~S|</details>|
594796 ]
595797 end
596798
799+ # The canonical leg buckets for the global findings list. Order matters —
800+ # this is the order groups appear in the rendered list.
801+ @findings_leg_order [
802+ "Spec well-formed",
803+ "Spec ↔ Code",
804+ "Spec ↔ Tests",
805+ "Code ↔ Tests",
806+ "Coverage",
807+ "Decisions",
808+ "Branch",
809+ "Other"
810+ ]
811+
812+ # Severity ordering within a group: errors first, warnings, info, then the
813+ # rest. Findings without a severity sort to the end.
814+ @severity_order ~w(error warning info)
815+
816+ @doc false
817+ def group_findings_by_leg(findings) do
818+ by_leg =
819+ Enum.reduce(findings, %{}, fn f, acc ->
820+ leg = leg_for_finding_code(f["code"])
821+ Map.update(acc, leg, [f], &[f | &1])
822+ end)
823+
824+ @findings_leg_order
825+ |> Enum.flat_map(fn leg ->
826+ case Map.get(by_leg, leg) do
827+ nil -> []
828+ items -> [{leg, sort_findings_by_severity(Enum.reverse(items))}]
829+ end
830+ end)
831+ end
832+
833+ # covers: specled.spec_review.triangle_code_classification
834+ # Map a finding code to its triangle leg by reusing the code-to-leg
835+ # mapping that already lives in build_sync_checks (the sync checklist's
836+ # source of truth, added by the triangle-code classifier work). Codes
837+ # the checklist does not classify fall into the "Other" bucket, which
838+ # still groups them at the bottom of the list.
839+ @doc false
840+ def leg_for_finding_code(code) when is_binary(code) do
841+ Map.get(code_to_leg_lookup(), code, "Other")
842+ end
843+
844+ def leg_for_finding_code(_), do: "Other"
845+
846+ # Walk build_sync_checks (with a zeroed triage — only `codes` and `leg`
847+ # matter for the lookup) and project each row's code list onto its leg
848+ # bucket. The first row to claim a code wins, which mirrors the order
849+ # that build_sync_checks emits. The detailed sync-check `leg` labels
850+ # ("Tests → Coverage", "Spec → Decisions", "Decisions / governance",
851+ # ...) are normalized to the seven public buckets used by this list.
852+ @doc false
853+ def code_to_leg_lookup do
854+ zeroed_triage = %{
855+ findings_by_code: %{},
856+ affected_subject_count: 0,
857+ binding_count: 0,
858+ requirement_count: 0,
859+ verification_count: 0,
860+ adr_ref_count: 0,
861+ strength_breakdown: %{}
862+ }
863+
864+ zeroed_triage
865+ |> build_sync_checks()
866+ |> Enum.flat_map(fn check ->
867+ bucket = sync_check_leg_to_bucket(check.leg)
868+ Enum.map(check.codes, fn code -> {code, bucket} end)
869+ end)
870+ |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn {code, bucket}, acc ->
871+ Map.put_new(acc, code, bucket)
872+ end)
873+ end
874+
875+ # Normalize the detailed sync-check leg labels to the seven public
876+ # buckets used by the global findings list. This is the one place where
877+ # the public group vocabulary differs from the internal sync-check
878+ # vocabulary.
879+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Spec"), do: "Spec well-formed"
880+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Spec → Code"), do: "Spec ↔ Code"
881+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Spec → Tests"), do: "Spec ↔ Tests"
882+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Code → Tests"), do: "Code ↔ Tests"
883+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Tests → Coverage"), do: "Coverage"
884+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Coverage"), do: "Coverage"
885+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Spec → Decisions"), do: "Decisions"
886+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Decisions / governance"), do: "Decisions"
887+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket("Branch"), do: "Branch"
888+ defp sync_check_leg_to_bucket(_), do: "Other"
889+
890+ defp sort_findings_by_severity(findings) do
891+ Enum.sort_by(findings, fn f ->
892+ sev = f["severity"] || ""
893+
894+ case Enum.find_index(@severity_order, &(&1 == sev)) do
895+ nil -> length(@severity_order)
896+ idx -> idx
897+ end
898+ end)
899+ end
900+
901+ defp render_findings_group({leg, findings}) do
902+ counts = severity_counts(findings)
903+ total = length(findings)
904+
905+ [
906+ ~s|<div class="findings-group" data-leg="#{h(leg)}">|,
907+ ~s|<h3 class="findings-group-heading">|,
908+ ~s|<span class="findings-group-leg">#{h(leg)}</span>|,
909+ ~s| <span class="findings-group-count">#{total} #{maybe_plural(total, "finding")}</span>|,
910+ render_findings_group_severity_chips(counts),
911+ ~S|</h3>|,
912+ ~s|<ul class="findings-group-list">|,
913+ Enum.map(findings, &render_finding_item/1),
914+ ~S|</ul>|,
915+ ~S|</div>|
916+ ]
917+ end
918+
919+ defp render_findings_group_severity_chips(counts) do
920+ @severity_order
921+ |> Enum.map(fn sev ->
922+ case Map.get(counts, sev, 0) do
923+ 0 ->
924+ ""
925+
926+ n ->
927+ ~s| <span class="findings-group-sev findings-group-sev-#{h(sev)}"#{title_attr(tooltip(:severity, sev))}>#{n} #{h(sev)}</span>|
928+ end
929+ end)
930+ end
931+
932+ defp severity_counts(findings) do
933+ Enum.reduce(findings, %{}, fn f, acc ->
934+ sev = f["severity"] || ""
935+ Map.update(acc, sev, 1, &(&1 + 1))
936+ end)
937+ end
938+
939+ defp render_finding_item(f) do
940+ sev = f["severity"] || ""
941+
942+ ~s"""
943+ <li class="finding-item finding-#{h(sev)}">
944+ <span class="finding-severity"#{title_attr(tooltip(:severity, sev))}>#{h(sev)}</span>
945+ <span class="finding-code">#{h(f["code"])}</span>
946+ #{render_subject_link(f)}
947+ <span class="finding-message">#{h(f["message"])}</span>
948+ </li>
949+ """
950+ end
951+
952+ # Render the subject anchor(s) shown in the All-findings row.
953+ #
954+ # For most finding codes the `subject_id` IS the subject the reviewer should
955+ # follow, so we render a single anchor. For `branch_guard_untethered_test`,
956+ # though, `subject_id` is the *claimed* subject (the one the test's `@tag
957+ # spec:` named) while the test's coverage actually exercises a different
958+ # subject — the `observed_owners` list. Linking only to the claimed subject
959+ # sends the reviewer to a card with no evidence of the misalignment. We
960+ # surface both: "claims A, hits B" with both names anchored, so the headline
961+ # IS the misalignment.
597962 defp render_subject_link(nil), do: ""
598963
599− defp render_subject_link(subject_id) do
964+ defp render_subject_link(%{"code" => "branch_guard_untethered_test"} = finding) do
965+ claimed = finding["subject_id"]
966+ observed = List.wrap(finding["observed_owners"]) |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
967+
968+ case {claimed, observed} do
969+ {nil, []} ->
970+ ""
971+
972+ {nil, _} ->
973+ # No claimed subject (shouldn't happen in practice, but render safely).
974+ render_subject_anchors(observed)
975+
976+ {_, []} ->
977+ # No observed owners recorded — fall back to the single claimed link.
978+ render_subject_anchor(claimed)
979+
980+ {_, _} ->
981+ ~s|<span class="finding-subject-pair">| <>
982+ ~s|<span class="finding-subject-label">claims</span> | <>
983+ render_subject_anchor(claimed) <>
984+ ~s|<span class="finding-subject-label">, hits</span> | <>
985+ render_subject_anchors(observed) <>
986+ ~s|</span>|
987+ end
988+ end
989+
990+ defp render_subject_link(%{"subject_id" => subject_id}), do: render_subject_link(subject_id)
991+
992+ defp render_subject_link(finding) when is_map(finding) do
993+ render_subject_link(Map.get(finding, "subject_id"))
994+ end
995+
996+ defp render_subject_link(subject_id) when is_binary(subject_id) do
997+ render_subject_anchor(subject_id)
998+ end
999+
1000+ defp render_subject_link(_), do: ""
1001+
1002+ defp render_subject_anchor(subject_id) when is_binary(subject_id) do
6001003 ~s|<a class="finding-subject" href="#subject-#{slug(subject_id)}">#{h(subject_id)}</a>|
6011004 end
6021005
1006+ defp render_subject_anchor(_), do: ""
1007+
1008+ defp render_subject_anchors(subject_ids) do
1009+ subject_ids
1010+ |> Enum.map(&render_subject_anchor/1)
1011+ |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
1012+ |> Enum.intersperse(", ")
1013+ |> Enum.join("")
1014+ end
1015+

6911105 ]
6921106 end
6931107
1108+ # covers: specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges
1109+ # Surface per-subject binding health on the card header so triage does not
1110+ # require a tab-dive into Coverage. Three states:
1111+ # * 0 bindings (no realized_by declared) — advisory chip
1112+ # * N bindings, K dangling — error chip when dangling > 0
1113+ # * N bindings, all valid — success chip when bindings > 0
1114+ # Dangling counts come from the per-subject findings list, so the badge stays
1115+ # in sync with the same `branch_guard_dangling_binding` finding rendered in
1116+ # the triage panel.
1117+ @doc false
1118+ def render_subject_binding_health(bindings, findings) do
1119+ total = count_bindings(bindings)
1120+ dangling = count_dangling_findings(findings)
1121+
1122+ cond do
1123+ total == 0 ->
1124+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-binding-health badge-binding-empty"#{title_attr(tooltip(:binding_health, :none))}>0 bindings (no realized_by declared)</span>|
1125+
1126+ dangling > 0 ->
1127+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-binding-health badge-binding-dangling"#{title_attr(tooltip(:binding_health, :dangling))}>#{total} #{maybe_plural(total, "binding")}, #{dangling} dangling</span>|
1128+
1129+ true ->
1130+ ~s|<span class="badge badge-binding-health badge-binding-valid"#{title_attr(tooltip(:binding_health, :valid))}>#{total} #{maybe_plural(total, "binding")}, all valid</span>|
1131+ end
1132+ end
1133+
1134+ defp count_bindings(nil), do: 0
1135+ defp count_bindings(bindings) when bindings == %{}, do: 0
1136+
1137+ defp count_bindings(bindings) when is_map(bindings) do
1138+ bindings
1139+ |> Map.values()
1140+ |> Enum.reduce(0, fn mfas, acc -> acc + length(List.wrap(mfas)) end)
1141+ end
1142+
1143+ defp count_bindings(_), do: 0
1144+
1145+ defp count_dangling_findings(findings) when is_list(findings) do
1146+ Enum.count(findings, fn f ->
1147+ Map.get(f, "code") == "branch_guard_dangling_binding" or
1148+ Map.get(f, :code) == "branch_guard_dangling_binding"
1149+ end)
1150+ end
1151+
1152+ defp count_dangling_findings(_), do: 0
1153+

6961156 req_status = id_status_lookup(s.spec_changes.requirements)

1469+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
1470+ # When the tracer manifest or per-test coverage artifact is missing the
1471+ # bind-closure view collapses to a single banner above the requirements
1472+ # list. The top-of-page degraded banner already advertises that the
1473+ # whole report is partial; this status note explains the consequence
1474+ # for the closure summary specifically (so a reader doesn't read empty
1475+ # closure rows as "tests cover nothing").
1476+ @doc false
1477+ def render_closure_reach_status(%{status: :no_coverage_artifact}) do
10081478 ~S"""
1009− <details class="coverage-help">
1479+ <p class="cov-closure-unavailable" role="status">
1480+ <strong>Coverage artifact unavailable.</strong>
1481+ Per-requirement bind-closure reach cannot be computed without
1482+ <code>.spec/_coverage/per_test.coverdata</code>. Run
1483+ <code>mix spec.cover.test</code> to enable this view.
1484+ </p>
1485+ """
1486+ end
1487+
1488+ def render_closure_reach_status(%{status: :no_tracer_manifest}) do
1489+ ~S"""
1490+ <p class="cov-closure-unavailable" role="status">
1491+ <strong>Binding closure unavailable.</strong>
1492+ The compiler tracer manifest is missing, so the realization closure
1493+ cannot be walked. Recompile the project to regenerate the tracer
1494+ side-manifest.
1495+ </p>
1496+ """
1497+ end
1498+
1499+ def render_closure_reach_status(_), do: ""
1500+

1503+ # one copy near the triage panel and each tab links to it.
1504+ defp render_coverage_help_link do
1505+ ~S|<p class="coverage-help-link"><a href="#coverage-help">How is requirement coverage computed?</a></p>|
1506+ end
1507+
1508+ # covers: specled.spec_review.triage_panel
1509+ # The legend used to live inside every subject Coverage tab. It now renders

1511+ defp render_coverage_help_disclosure do
1512+ ~S"""
1513+ <details class="coverage-help" id="coverage-help">
10101514 <summary class="coverage-help-summary"><span aria-hidden="true">ℹ</span> How is requirement coverage computed?</summary>
10111515 <div class="coverage-help-body">
10121516 <p>Each requirement is verified by one or more <code>spec-verification</code> entries that name it in their <code>covers:</code> list. The verifier inspects each entry and reports the <strong>strongest evidence</strong> it could find for the requirement.</p>
@@ -1027,9 +1531,12 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
10271531 """
10281532 end

1573+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
1574+ # Per-requirement bind-closure summary rendered beneath each requirement
1575+ # in the Coverage tab. Format: "Closure: N MFAs. Reached: M (by tests
1576+ # T1, T2). Unreached: K." When the artifact is missing the renderer
1577+ # falls through to render_closure_reach_status/1 at the tab level and
1578+ # this helper renders nothing per-row to avoid duplicate noise.
1579+ defp render_closure_reach(_reach, :no_coverage_artifact), do: ""
1580+ defp render_closure_reach(_reach, :no_tracer_manifest), do: ""
1581+ defp render_closure_reach(nil, _), do: ""
1582+
1583+ defp render_closure_reach(%{closure_mfa_count: 0, closure_file_count: 0}, _) do
1584+ ~S|<p class="cov-closure" data-empty="true"><span class="cov-closure-label">Closure:</span> 0 MFAs.</p>|
1585+ end
1586+
1587+ defp render_closure_reach(reach, _) do
1588+ %{
1589+ closure_mfa_count: mfa_count,
1590+ reached_files: reached,
1591+ unreached_files: unreached,
1592+ reaching_tests: tests
1593+ } = reach
1594+
1595+ reached_count = length(reached)
1596+ unreached_count = length(unreached)
1597+
1598+ tests_segment =
1599+ case tests do
1600+ [] -> ""
1601+ list -> " (by tests #{render_reaching_tests(list)})"
1602+ end
1603+
1604+ ~s"""
1605+ <p class="cov-closure">
1606+ <span class="cov-closure-label">Closure:</span> #{mfa_count} MFA#{plural(mfa_count)}.
1607+ <span class="cov-closure-reached">Reached: #{reached_count}#{tests_segment}.</span>
1608+ <span class="cov-closure-unreached">Unreached: #{unreached_count}.</span>
1609+ </p>
1610+ """
1611+ end
1612+
1613+ defp render_reaching_tests(tests) do
1614+ tests
1615+ |> Enum.map(fn t -> ~s|<code class="cov-closure-test">#{h(t)}</code>| end)
1616+ |> Enum.join(", ")
1617+ end
1618+
1619+ defp plural(1), do: ""
1620+ defp plural(_), do: "s"
1621+

@@ -1149,39 +1707,66 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do

1710+ # Page-level aggregator. Renders a single "Raw verification (all subjects)"
1711+ # disclosure containing every spec-verification entry across affected
1712+ # subjects, grouped by subject. The per-subject Coverage tab no longer
1713+ # repeats this block — power-user noise is consolidated to one place.
1714+ defp render_raw_verification_all(subjects) do

1716+ subjects

1718+ |> Enum.reject(fn {_s, list} -> list == [] end)

1155− defp render_verification_section(list) when is_list(list) do

1722+ ""
1723+
1724+ _ ->

1726+
1727+ [
1728+ ~s|<section id="raw-verification" class="raw-verification-all" aria-label="Raw verification across subjects">|,
1729+ ~s|<details class="raw-verification">|,
1730+ ~s|<summary class="raw-verification-summary">Raw spec-verification (all subjects · #{total} entr#{if total == 1, do: "y", else: "ies"})</summary>|,
1731+ ~S|<p class="raw-verification-explainer">The author's declarations as written in each subject's spec file. The per-requirement Coverage views above are computed from these entries.</p>|,
1732+ Enum.map(grouped, fn {s, list} -> render_raw_verification_subject(s, list) end),
1733+ ~S|</details>|,
1734+ ~S|</section>|
1735+ ]
1736+ end
1737+ end
1738+

11601743 ~S|<ul class="verification-list">|,
1161− Enum.map(list, fn v ->
1162− kind = field(v, :kind) || ""

1166−
1167− ~s"""

1171− <code class="verification-target">#{h(target)}</code>
1172− #{if execute, do: ~s|<span class="pill pill-success"#{title_attr(tooltip(:execute, true))}>executes</span>|, else: ~s|<span class="pill pill-muted"#{title_attr(tooltip(:execute, false))}>declared</span>|}
1173− </div>
1174− <div class="verification-covers">

1751+ kind = field(v, :kind) || ""

1755+

1762+ </div>

1270− defp render_decisions_changed([], _adrs_by_id) do
1271− ~S"""
1272− <section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">
1273− <h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (0)</h2>
1274− <p class="empty-tab">No ADR files changed in this change set.</p>
1275− </section>
1276− """
1855+ # covers: specled.spec_review.decisions_governance_inline
1856+ # The Decisions Changed panel is no longer purely descriptive. Append-only
1857+ # governance findings (`append_only/*`) ride into the same section so that
1858+ # an unauthorized requirement deletion or an ADR-bound violation surfaces
1859+ # *next to* the ADR record where the reviewer is already focused. Findings
1860+ # whose entity_id resolves to a present ADR render under that ADR's card;
1861+ # the rest land in a "Governance violations" subsection at the top of the
1862+ # panel so they are not silently dropped when no ADR file changed.
1863+ @doc false
1864+ def render_decisions_changed(decisions, adrs_by_id, findings \\ [])
1865+
1866+ def render_decisions_changed([], adrs_by_id, findings) do
1867+ governance = governance_findings(findings)
1868+ {orphan_findings, _by_adr} = partition_governance_findings(governance, adrs_by_id || %{})
1869+
1870+ case {governance, orphan_findings} do
1871+ {[], _} ->
1872+ ~S"""
1873+ <section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">
1874+ <h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (0)</h2>
1875+ <p class="empty-tab">No ADR files changed in this change set.</p>
1876+ </section>
1877+ """
1878+
1879+ _ ->
1880+ [
1881+ ~s|<section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">|,
1882+ ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (0)</h2>|,
1883+ render_governance_orphans(orphan_findings),
1884+ ~s|<p class="empty-tab">No ADR files changed in this change set.</p>|,
1885+ ~S|</section>|
1886+ ]
1887+ end
12771888 end
12781889
1279− defp render_decisions_changed(decisions, adrs_by_id) do
1890+ def render_decisions_changed(decisions, adrs_by_id, findings) do
1891+ governance = governance_findings(findings)
1892+ adrs_by_id = adrs_by_id || %{}
1893+ {orphan_findings, by_adr} = partition_governance_findings(governance, adrs_by_id)
1894+
12801895 [
12811896 ~s|<section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">|,
12821897 ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (#{length(decisions)})</h2>|,
1898+ render_governance_orphans(orphan_findings),
12831899 ~S|<div class="decision-changed-list">|,
1284− Enum.map(decisions, &render_changed_decision(&1, adrs_by_id || %{})),
1900+ Enum.map(decisions, &render_changed_decision(&1, adrs_by_id, by_adr)),
12851901 ~S|</div>|,
12861902 ~S|</section>|
12871903 ]
12881904 end
12891905
1290− defp render_changed_decision(d, adrs_by_id) do
1291− case Map.get(adrs_by_id, d.id) do
1292− nil -> render_changed_decision_minimal(d)
1293− adr -> render_changed_decision_full(d, adr)
1906+ # An append_only/* finding tied to a known ADR id (e.g. adr_affects_widened
1907+ # naming an ADR that's in adrs_by_id) renders under that ADR's card. All
1908+ # other governance findings — unauthorized requirement deletions, decision
1909+ # deletions of ADRs no longer present, etc. — surface at the top of the
1910+ # panel as orphan governance violations.
1911+ defp partition_governance_findings(findings, adrs_by_id) do
1912+ Enum.reduce(findings, {[], %{}}, fn f, {orphans, by_adr} ->
1913+ entity_id = f["entity_id"]
1914+
1915+ cond do
1916+ is_binary(entity_id) and Map.has_key?(adrs_by_id, entity_id) ->
1917+ {orphans, Map.update(by_adr, entity_id, [f], &[f | &1])}
1918+
1919+ true ->
1920+ {[f | orphans], by_adr}
1921+ end
1922+ end)
1923+ |> then(fn {orphans, by_adr} ->
1924+ {Enum.reverse(orphans), Map.new(by_adr, fn {k, v} -> {k, Enum.reverse(v)} end)}
1925+ end)
1926+ end
1927+
1928+ defp governance_findings(findings) do
1929+ findings
1930+ |> List.wrap()
1931+ |> Enum.filter(fn f ->
1932+ code = f["code"] || ""
1933+ String.starts_with?(code, "append_only/")
1934+ end)
1935+ end
1936+
1937+ defp render_governance_orphans([]), do: ""
1938+
1939+ defp render_governance_orphans(findings) do
1940+ [
1941+ ~s|<details class="governance-orphans" open>|,
1942+ ~s|<summary class="governance-orphans-summary">Governance violations (#{length(findings)})</summary>|,
1943+ ~s|<p class="governance-orphans-explainer">These <code>append_only/*</code> findings are not authorized by any ADR in this change set. Each one is the spec system telling you a weakening landed without the decision trail it needs.</p>|,
1944+ ~s|<ul class="governance-finding-list">|,
1945+ Enum.map(findings, &render_governance_finding/1),
1946+ ~s|</ul>|,
1947+ ~s|</details>|
1948+ ]
1949+ end
1950+
1951+ defp render_changed_decision(d, adrs_by_id, governance_by_adr) do
1952+ findings = Map.get(governance_by_adr, d.id, [])
1953+
1954+ body =
1955+ case Map.get(adrs_by_id, d.id) do
1956+ nil -> render_changed_decision_minimal(d)
1957+ adr -> render_changed_decision_full(d, adr)
1958+ end
1959+
1960+ case findings do
1961+ [] -> body
1962+ _ -> [body, render_inline_governance_findings(findings)]
12941963 end
12951964 end
12961965
@@ -1343,6 +2012,54 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
13432012 """
13442013 end
13452014
2015+ defp render_inline_governance_findings(findings) do
2016+ [
2017+ ~s|<div class="governance-finding-inline">|,
2018+ ~s|<p class="governance-finding-inline-label">Governance findings naming this ADR:</p>|,
2019+ ~s|<ul class="governance-finding-list">|,
2020+ Enum.map(findings, &render_governance_finding/1),
2021+ ~s|</ul>|,
2022+ ~s|</div>|
2023+ ]
2024+ end
2025+
2026+ defp render_governance_finding(f) do
2027+ sev = f["severity"] || "error"
2028+ code = f["code"] || ""
2029+ message = f["message"] || ""
2030+ {prose, fix_block} = split_fix_block(message)
2031+
2032+ fix_html =
2033+ case fix_block do
2034+ nil -> ""
2035+ text -> ~s|<pre class="governance-finding-fix"><code>#{h(text)}</code></pre>|
2036+ end
2037+
2038+ ~s"""
2039+ <li class="governance-finding finding-#{h(sev)}">
2040+ <div class="governance-finding-header">
2041+ <span class="finding-severity"#{title_attr(tooltip(:severity, sev))}>#{h(sev)}</span>
2042+ <span class="finding-code">#{h(code)}</span>
2043+ #{render_subject_link(f["subject_id"])}
2044+ </div>
2045+ <p class="governance-finding-message">#{h(prose)}</p>
2046+ #{fix_html}
2047+ </li>
2048+ """
2049+ end
2050+
2051+ # AppendOnly messages are produced by `finalize_message/2` as
2052+ # "<prose>\n\n```\nfix: ...\n```\n". We split them so the prose flows as
2053+ # readable text and the fix line keeps its monospace, code-fenced shape.
2054+ defp split_fix_block(message) when is_binary(message) do
2055+ case Regex.run(~r/^(.*?)\n*```\n(fix:[^\n]*(?:\n[^\n]*)*)\n```\s*$/s, message) do
2056+ [_, prose, fix_text] -> {String.trim_trailing(prose), String.trim(fix_text)}
2057+ _ -> {message, nil}
2058+ end
2059+ end
2060+
2061+ defp split_fix_block(other), do: {to_string(other), nil}
2062+
13462063 defp render_decision_status_pill(nil), do: ""
13472064
13482065 defp render_decision_status_pill(status) do
@@ -1402,7 +2119,6 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
14022119
14032120 defp render_unmapped(changes, breakdown) do
14042121 paths = Enum.map(changes, & &1.file)
1405− tree = build_path_tree(paths)
14062122
14072123 diff_blocks =
14082124 Enum.map(changes, fn %{file: file, lines: lines} ->
@@ -1421,11 +2137,31 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
14212137 ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Outside the spec system (#{length(changes)})</h2>|,
14222138 render_misc_breakdown(breakdown),
14232139 ~S|<p class="misc-explainer">These files changed but do not map to any spec subject. Triangulation does not apply here — review the diff directly.</p>|,
1424− render_files_section(tree, "misc", "Files (#{length(paths)})", diff_blocks),
2140+ render_misc_files(paths, diff_blocks),
14252141 ~S|</section>|
14262142 ]
14272143 end
14282144
2145+ # When there are few unmapped files (<= 3), the file-tree rail is more
2146+ # chrome than signal — render the diff blocks flat. Above that threshold,
2147+ # fall back to the standard tree+files layout.
2148+ @misc_flat_threshold 3
2149+
2150+ defp render_misc_files(paths, diff_blocks) when length(paths) <= @misc_flat_threshold do
2151+ ~s"""
2152+ <div class="files-section files-section-flat">
2153+ <div class="files-list">
2154+ #{IO.iodata_to_binary(diff_blocks)}
2155+ </div>
2156+ </div>
2157+ """
2158+ end
2159+
2160+ defp render_misc_files(paths, diff_blocks) do
2161+ tree = build_path_tree(paths)
2162+ render_files_section(tree, "misc", "Files (#{length(paths)})", diff_blocks)
2163+ end
2164+
14292165 # Header breakdown so a reviewer can see all N changed files accounted
14302166 # for: how many landed in subjects (visible in their Code tabs above),
14312167 # how many are spec/decision policy files (visible in their Spec tabs
@@ -1791,6 +2527,17 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
17912527 defp tooltip(:severity, "info"),
17922528 do: "Info — advisory; never blocks."
17932529
2530+ defp tooltip(:binding_health, :none),
2531+ do:
2532+ "No realized_by bindings declared on this subject. Coverage strength tops out at LINKED until bindings are added."
2533+
2534+ defp tooltip(:binding_health, :dangling),
2535+ do:
2536+ "One or more realized_by bindings could not be resolved to actual code. See the dangling-binding findings in the triage panel."
2537+
2538+ defp tooltip(:binding_health, :valid),
2539+ do: "All declared realized_by bindings resolve to actual code on this branch."
2540+
17942541 defp tooltip(_, _), do: ""
17952542
17962543 defp title_attr(text) when is_binary(text) and text != "",
@@ -2218,6 +2965,13 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
22182965 }
22192966 .sync-edge-fail .sync-edge-line { background: #ea580c; }
22202967 .sync-edge-fail .sync-edge-line::after { border-left-color: #ea580c; }
2968+ /* Degraded edge: detector_unavailable on this leg. Solid amber/gray
2969+ — neither a positive proof nor a failure; the system is being
2970+ honest that the detector could not run. */
2971+ .sync-edge-degraded .sync-edge-line { background: #d4a373; }

@@ -2308,10 +3062,12 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
