1270− defp render_decisions_changed([], _adrs_by_id) do
1271− ~S"""
1272− <section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">
1273− <h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (0)</h2>
1274− <p class="empty-tab">No ADR files changed in this change set.</p>
1275− </section>
1276− """
1855+ # covers: specled.spec_review.decisions_governance_inline
1856+ # The Decisions Changed panel is no longer purely descriptive. Append-only
1857+ # governance findings (`append_only/*`) ride into the same section so that
1858+ # an unauthorized requirement deletion or an ADR-bound violation surfaces
1859+ # *next to* the ADR record where the reviewer is already focused. Findings
1860+ # whose entity_id resolves to a present ADR render under that ADR's card;
1861+ # the rest land in a "Governance violations" subsection at the top of the
1862+ # panel so they are not silently dropped when no ADR file changed.
1863+ @doc false
1864+ def render_decisions_changed(decisions, adrs_by_id, findings \\ [])
1865+
1866+ def render_decisions_changed([], adrs_by_id, findings) do
1867+ governance = governance_findings(findings)
1868+ {orphan_findings, _by_adr} = partition_governance_findings(governance, adrs_by_id || %{})
1869+
1870+ case {governance, orphan_findings} do
1871+ {[], _} ->
1872+ ~S"""
1873+ <section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">
1874+ <h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (0)</h2>
1875+ <p class="empty-tab">No ADR files changed in this change set.</p>
1876+ </section>
1877+ """
1878+
1879+ _ ->
1880+ [
1881+ ~s|<section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">|,
1882+ ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (0)</h2>|,
1883+ render_governance_orphans(orphan_findings),
1884+ ~s|<p class="empty-tab">No ADR files changed in this change set.</p>|,
1885+ ~S|</section>|
1886+ ]
1887+ end
12771888 end
12781889
1279− defp render_decisions_changed(decisions, adrs_by_id) do
1890+ def render_decisions_changed(decisions, adrs_by_id, findings) do
1891+ governance = governance_findings(findings)
1892+ adrs_by_id = adrs_by_id || %{}
1893+ {orphan_findings, by_adr} = partition_governance_findings(governance, adrs_by_id)
1894+
12801895 [
12811896 ~s|<section id="decisions-changed" class="decisions-changed" aria-label="Decisions changed">|,
12821897 ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Decisions changed (#{length(decisions)})</h2>|,
1898+ render_governance_orphans(orphan_findings),
12831899 ~S|<div class="decision-changed-list">|,
1284− Enum.map(decisions, &render_changed_decision(&1, adrs_by_id || %{})),
1900+ Enum.map(decisions, &render_changed_decision(&1, adrs_by_id, by_adr)),
12851901 ~S|</div>|,
12861902 ~S|</section>|
12871903 ]
12881904 end
12891905
1290− defp render_changed_decision(d, adrs_by_id) do
1291− case Map.get(adrs_by_id, d.id) do
1292− nil -> render_changed_decision_minimal(d)
1293− adr -> render_changed_decision_full(d, adr)
1906+ # An append_only/* finding tied to a known ADR id (e.g. adr_affects_widened
1907+ # naming an ADR that's in adrs_by_id) renders under that ADR's card. All
1908+ # other governance findings — unauthorized requirement deletions, decision
1909+ # deletions of ADRs no longer present, etc. — surface at the top of the
1910+ # panel as orphan governance violations.
1911+ defp partition_governance_findings(findings, adrs_by_id) do
1912+ Enum.reduce(findings, {[], %{}}, fn f, {orphans, by_adr} ->
1913+ entity_id = f["entity_id"]
1914+
1915+ cond do
1916+ is_binary(entity_id) and Map.has_key?(adrs_by_id, entity_id) ->
1917+ {orphans, Map.update(by_adr, entity_id, [f], &[f | &1])}
1918+
1919+ true ->
1920+ {[f | orphans], by_adr}
1921+ end
1922+ end)
1923+ |> then(fn {orphans, by_adr} ->
1924+ {Enum.reverse(orphans), Map.new(by_adr, fn {k, v} -> {k, Enum.reverse(v)} end)}
1925+ end)
1926+ end
1927+
1928+ defp governance_findings(findings) do
1929+ findings
1930+ |> List.wrap()
1931+ |> Enum.filter(fn f ->
1932+ code = f["code"] || ""
1933+ String.starts_with?(code, "append_only/")
1934+ end)
1935+ end
1936+
1937+ defp render_governance_orphans([]), do: ""
1938+
1939+ defp render_governance_orphans(findings) do
1940+ [
1941+ ~s|<details class="governance-orphans" open>|,
1942+ ~s|<summary class="governance-orphans-summary">Governance violations (#{length(findings)})</summary>|,
1943+ ~s|<p class="governance-orphans-explainer">These <code>append_only/*</code> findings are not authorized by any ADR in this change set. Each one is the spec system telling you a weakening landed without the decision trail it needs.</p>|,
1944+ ~s|<ul class="governance-finding-list">|,
1945+ Enum.map(findings, &render_governance_finding/1),
1946+ ~s|</ul>|,
1947+ ~s|</details>|
1948+ ]
1949+ end
1950+
1951+ defp render_changed_decision(d, adrs_by_id, governance_by_adr) do
1952+ findings = Map.get(governance_by_adr, d.id, [])
1953+
1954+ body =
1955+ case Map.get(adrs_by_id, d.id) do
1956+ nil -> render_changed_decision_minimal(d)
1957+ adr -> render_changed_decision_full(d, adr)
1958+ end
1959+
1960+ case findings do
1961+ [] -> body
1962+ _ -> [body, render_inline_governance_findings(findings)]
12941963 end
12951964 end
12961965
@@ -1343,6 +2012,54 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
13432012 """
13442013 end
13452014
2015+ defp render_inline_governance_findings(findings) do
2016+ [
2017+ ~s|<div class="governance-finding-inline">|,
2018+ ~s|<p class="governance-finding-inline-label">Governance findings naming this ADR:</p>|,
2019+ ~s|<ul class="governance-finding-list">|,
2020+ Enum.map(findings, &render_governance_finding/1),
2021+ ~s|</ul>|,
2022+ ~s|</div>|
2023+ ]
2024+ end
2025+
2026+ defp render_governance_finding(f) do
2027+ sev = f["severity"] || "error"
2028+ code = f["code"] || ""
2029+ message = f["message"] || ""
2030+ {prose, fix_block} = split_fix_block(message)
2031+
2032+ fix_html =
2033+ case fix_block do
2034+ nil -> ""
2035+ text -> ~s|<pre class="governance-finding-fix"><code>#{h(text)}</code></pre>|
2036+ end
2037+
2038+ ~s"""
2039+ <li class="governance-finding finding-#{h(sev)}">
2040+ <div class="governance-finding-header">
2041+ <span class="finding-severity"#{title_attr(tooltip(:severity, sev))}>#{h(sev)}</span>
2042+ <span class="finding-code">#{h(code)}</span>
2043+ #{render_subject_link(f["subject_id"])}
2044+ </div>
2045+ <p class="governance-finding-message">#{h(prose)}</p>
2046+ #{fix_html}
2047+ </li>
2048+ """
2049+ end
2050+
2051+ # AppendOnly messages are produced by `finalize_message/2` as
2052+ # "<prose>\n\n```\nfix: ...\n```\n". We split them so the prose flows as
2053+ # readable text and the fix line keeps its monospace, code-fenced shape.
2054+ defp split_fix_block(message) when is_binary(message) do
2055+ case Regex.run(~r/^(.*?)\n*```\n(fix:[^\n]*(?:\n[^\n]*)*)\n```\s*$/s, message) do
2056+ [_, prose, fix_text] -> {String.trim_trailing(prose), String.trim(fix_text)}
2057+ _ -> {message, nil}
2058+ end
2059+ end
2060+
2061+ defp split_fix_block(other), do: {to_string(other), nil}
2062+
13462063 defp render_decision_status_pill(nil), do: ""
13472064
13482065 defp render_decision_status_pill(status) do
@@ -1402,7 +2119,6 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
14022119
14032120 defp render_unmapped(changes, breakdown) do
14042121 paths = Enum.map(changes, & &1.file)
1405− tree = build_path_tree(paths)
14062122
14072123 diff_blocks =
14082124 Enum.map(changes, fn %{file: file, lines: lines} ->
@@ -1421,11 +2137,31 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
14212137 ~s|<h2 class="section-heading">Outside the spec system (#{length(changes)})</h2>|,
14222138 render_misc_breakdown(breakdown),
14232139 ~S|<p class="misc-explainer">These files changed but do not map to any spec subject. Triangulation does not apply here — review the diff directly.</p>|,
1424− render_files_section(tree, "misc", "Files (#{length(paths)})", diff_blocks),
2140+ render_misc_files(paths, diff_blocks),
14252141 ~S|</section>|
14262142 ]
14272143 end
14282144
2145+ # When there are few unmapped files (<= 3), the file-tree rail is more
2146+ # chrome than signal — render the diff blocks flat. Above that threshold,
2147+ # fall back to the standard tree+files layout.
2148+ @misc_flat_threshold 3
2149+
2150+ defp render_misc_files(paths, diff_blocks) when length(paths) <= @misc_flat_threshold do
2151+ ~s"""
2152+ <div class="files-section files-section-flat">
2153+ <div class="files-list">
2154+ #{IO.iodata_to_binary(diff_blocks)}
2155+ </div>
2156+ </div>
2157+ """
2158+ end
2159+
2160+ defp render_misc_files(paths, diff_blocks) do
2161+ tree = build_path_tree(paths)
2162+ render_files_section(tree, "misc", "Files (#{length(paths)})", diff_blocks)
2163+ end
2164+
14292165 # Header breakdown so a reviewer can see all N changed files accounted

14312167 # how many are spec/decision policy files (visible in their Spec tabs
@@ -1791,6 +2527,17 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do

17932529

2531+ do:
2532+ "No realized_by bindings declared on this subject. Coverage strength tops out at LINKED until bindings are added."
2533+
2534+ defp tooltip(:binding_health, :dangling),
2535+ do:
2536+ "One or more realized_by bindings could not be resolved to actual code. See the dangling-binding findings in the triage panel."
2537+

2540+
17942541 defp tooltip(_, _), do: ""
17952542
17962543 defp title_attr(text) when is_binary(text) and text != "",
@@ -2218,6 +2965,13 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do
22182965 }
22192966 .sync-edge-fail .sync-edge-line { background: #ea580c; }
22202967 .sync-edge-fail .sync-edge-line::after { border-left-color: #ea580c; }
2968+ /* Degraded edge: detector_unavailable on this leg. Solid amber/gray
2969+ — neither a positive proof nor a failure; the system is being
2970+ honest that the detector could not run. */
2971+ .sync-edge-degraded .sync-edge-line { background: #d4a373; }
2972+ .sync-edge-degraded .sync-edge-line::after { border-left-color: #d4a373; }
2973+ .sync-edge-degraded .sync-edge-icon { color: #b45309; }
2974+ .sync-edge-degraded .sync-edge-label { color: #92400e; }
22212975 /* Vacuous edge: nothing to verify on this leg. Dashed gray — neither
22222976 a positive proof nor a failure. */

@@ -2308,10 +3062,12 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.Html do

test/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation_test.exs
@@ -308,6 +308,89 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.CoverageTriangulationTest do
308308 end
309309 end
310310
311+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
312+ describe "per_requirement_reach/2" do
313+ test "returns :no_coverage_artifact when given the sentinel" do
314+ assert CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach(:no_coverage_artifact, fixture_closure_map()) ==
315+ :no_coverage_artifact
316+ end
317+
318+ test "reports closure mfa+file counts for every requirement, even when no test reaches it" do
319+ reach = CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach([], fixture_closure_map())
320+
321+ a_req1 = reach[{"subject_a", "subject_a.req1"}]
322+ assert a_req1.closure_mfa_count == 1
323+ assert a_req1.closure_file_count == 1
324+ assert a_req1.reached_files == []
325+ assert a_req1.unreached_files == ["lib/a.ex"]
326+ assert a_req1.reaching_tests == []
327+ end
328+
329+ test "names the tests that reach each requirement's closure" do
330+ records = [
331+ coverage_record(
332+ test_id: "A.t1",
333+ file: "lib/a.ex",
334+ lines_hit: [1],
335+ tags: %{file: "test/a_test.exs", test: "covers req1"}
336+ )
337+ ]
338+
339+ reach = CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach(records, fixture_closure_map())
340+
341+ a_req1 = reach[{"subject_a", "subject_a.req1"}]
342+ assert a_req1.reached_files == ["lib/a.ex"]
343+ assert a_req1.unreached_files == []
344+ assert a_req1.reaching_tests == ["test/a_test.exs :: covers req1"]
345+
346+ # subject_a.req2's closure (lib/a_extra.ex) was not reached.
347+ a_req2 = reach[{"subject_a", "subject_a.req2"}]
348+ assert a_req2.reached_files == []
349+ assert a_req2.unreached_files == ["lib/a_extra.ex"]
350+ assert a_req2.reaching_tests == []
351+ end
352+
353+ test "deduplicates and sorts test display names across multiple closure files" do
354+ records = [
355+ coverage_record(
356+ test_id: "A.t1",
357+ file: "lib/a.ex",
358+ lines_hit: [1],
359+ tags: %{file: "test/a_test.exs", test: "alpha"}
360+ ),
361+ coverage_record(
362+ test_id: "B.t1",
363+ file: "lib/a.ex",
364+ lines_hit: [1],
365+ tags: %{file: "test/b_test.exs", test: "beta"}
366+ )
367+ ]
368+
369+ reach = CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach(records, fixture_closure_map())
370+ a_req1 = reach[{"subject_a", "subject_a.req1"}]
371+
372+ assert a_req1.reaching_tests == [
373+ "test/a_test.exs :: alpha",
374+ "test/b_test.exs :: beta"
375+ ]
376+ end
377+
378+ test "skips records whose lines_hit is empty (no actual coverage)" do
379+ records = [
380+ coverage_record(
381+ test_id: "A.t_empty",
382+ file: "lib/a.ex",
383+ lines_hit: [],
384+ tags: %{file: "test/a_test.exs", test: "no-hits"}
385+ )
386+ ]
387+
388+ reach = CoverageTriangulation.per_requirement_reach(records, fixture_closure_map())
389+ assert reach[{"subject_a", "subject_a.req1"}].reaching_tests == []
390+ assert reach[{"subject_a", "subject_a.req1"}].reached_files == []
391+ end
392+ end
393+
311394 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
312395 # Fixtures
313396 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
test/specled_ex/review/html_test.exs
@@ -0,0 +1,1106 @@
1+defmodule SpecLedEx.Review.HtmlTest do
2+ # Triangle leg classifier + sync checklist tests.
3+ #
4+ # The leg classifier (render_sync_diagram) and the sync checklist
5+ # (build_sync_checks) translate finding codes into per-leg statuses.
6+ # These tests pin the triangle vocabulary documented in
7+ # docs/concepts.md to the diagram + checklist legs they belong to,
8+ # so a fixture-style triage carrying any one of those codes flips the
9+ # correct row from green to amber.
10+
11+ use ExUnit.Case, async: true
12+
13+ alias SpecLedEx.Review.Html
14+
15+ defp triage(findings_by_code, overrides \\ %{}) do
16+ Map.merge(
17+ %{
18+ affected_subject_count: 1,
19+ requirement_count: 2,
20+ binding_count: 3,
21+ verification_count: 1,
22+ adr_ref_count: 0,
23+ strength_breakdown: %{},
24+ findings_by_code: findings_by_code,
25+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{}
26+ },
27+ overrides
28+ )
29+ end
30+
31+ defp checks_by_codes(checks) do
32+ Enum.reduce(checks, %{}, fn check, acc ->
33+ Enum.reduce(check.codes, acc, fn code, inner ->
34+ Map.update(inner, code, [check], &[check | &1])
35+ end)
36+ end)
37+ end
38+
39+ # Pull the labels of edges in a given state. The diagram renders each
40+ # edge as `<div class="sync-edge sync-edge-<state>" ...>...<span ...>icon</span>
41+ # <label></div>`. We need the label text to verify which side flipped.
42+ defp extract_labels(html, state) do
43+ pattern = ~r|sync-edge sync-edge-#{state}.*?sync-edge-icon">[^<]*</span>\s*([^<]+)</div>|s
44+
45+ Regex.scan(pattern, html, capture: :all_but_first)
46+ |> List.flatten()
47+ |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
48+ end
49+
50+ describe "render_sync_diagram leg classifier — SPEC ↔ CODE" do
51+ for code <- ~w(branch_guard_realization_drift branch_guard_dangling_binding) do
52+ @code code
53+ test "#{@code} flips the realized_by edge from ok to fail" do
54+ clean = triage(%{})
55+ dirty = triage(%{@code => 1})
56+
57+ assert "realized_by" in extract_labels(
58+ IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(clean)),
59+ "ok"
60+ )
61+
62+ assert "realized_by" in extract_labels(
63+ IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(dirty)),
64+ "fail"
65+ )
66+ end
67+ end
68+ end
69+
70+ describe "render_sync_diagram leg classifier — SPEC ↔ TESTS" do
71+ for code <- ~w(
72+ branch_guard_untested_realization
73+ requirement_without_test_tag
74+ branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag
75+ ) do
76+ @code code
77+ test "#{@code} flips the @tag spec edge from ok to fail" do
78+ dirty = triage(%{@code => 1})
79+
80+ # The "@tag spec" edge is the diagram's Code → Tests edge by
81+ # position; in the triangle vocabulary it represents SPEC ↔ TESTS.
82+ assert "@tag spec" in extract_labels(
83+ IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(dirty)),
84+ "fail"
85+ )
86+ end
87+ end
88+ end
89+
90+ describe "render_sync_diagram leg classifier — CODE ↔ TESTS" do
91+ for code <- ~w(branch_guard_untethered_test branch_guard_underspecified_realization) do
92+ @code code
93+ test "#{@code} flips the evidence edge from ok to fail" do
94+ dirty = triage(%{@code => 1}, %{strength_breakdown: %{executed: 1}})
95+
96+ assert "evidence" in extract_labels(
97+ IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(dirty)),
98+ "fail"
99+ )
100+ end
101+ end
102+ end
103+
104+ describe "build_sync_checks — Spec well-formed row" do
105+ test "overlap/duplicate_covers fails the 'spec files are well-formed' check" do
106+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{"overlap/duplicate_covers" => 1}))
107+ spec_check = Enum.find(checks, &(&1.label == "The spec files themselves are well-formed"))
108+
109+ assert spec_check
110+ assert spec_check.passed? == false
111+ assert "overlap/duplicate_covers" in spec_check.codes
112+ end
113+
114+ test "overlap/must_stem_collision fails the 'spec files are well-formed' check" do
115+ checks =
116+ Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{"overlap/must_stem_collision" => 1}))
117+
118+ spec_check = Enum.find(checks, &(&1.label == "The spec files themselves are well-formed"))
119+
120+ assert spec_check
121+ assert spec_check.passed? == false
122+ assert "overlap/must_stem_collision" in spec_check.codes
123+ end
124+ end
125+
126+ describe "build_sync_checks — Decisions / governance row" do
127+ @append_only_codes ~w(
128+ append_only/requirement_deleted
129+ append_only/must_downgraded
130+ append_only/scenario_regression
131+ append_only/negative_removed
132+ append_only/disabled_without_reason
133+ append_only/no_baseline
134+ append_only/adr_affects_widened
135+ append_only/same_pr_self_authorization
136+ append_only/missing_change_type
137+ append_only/decision_deleted
138+ )
139+
140+ test "the row exists with leg label 'Decisions / governance'" do
141+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{}))
142+ gov = Enum.find(checks, &(&1.leg == "Decisions / governance"))
143+
144+ assert gov, "expected a 'Decisions / governance' row to exist"
145+ end
146+
147+ test "the row covers every append_only/* code in the catalog" do
148+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{}))
149+ gov = Enum.find(checks, &(&1.leg == "Decisions / governance"))
150+
151+ for code <- @append_only_codes do
152+ assert code in gov.codes,
153+ "Decisions / governance row missing append_only code: #{code}"
154+ end
155+ end
156+
157+ for code <- ~w(
158+ append_only/requirement_deleted
159+ append_only/must_downgraded
160+ append_only/scenario_regression
161+ append_only/negative_removed
162+ append_only/disabled_without_reason
163+ append_only/no_baseline
164+ append_only/adr_affects_widened
165+ append_only/same_pr_self_authorization
166+ append_only/missing_change_type
167+ append_only/decision_deleted
168+ ) do
169+ @code code
170+ test "#{@code} flips the 'Decisions / governance' row from passed to failed" do
171+ clean = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{}))
172+ dirty = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{@code => 1}))
173+
174+ clean_gov = Enum.find(clean, &(&1.leg == "Decisions / governance"))
175+ dirty_gov = Enum.find(dirty, &(&1.leg == "Decisions / governance"))
176+
177+ assert clean_gov.passed? == true
178+ assert dirty_gov.passed? == false
179+ assert dirty_gov.count == 1
180+ end
181+ end
182+ end
183+
184+ describe "build_sync_checks — Spec → Code triangle row" do
185+ for code <- ~w(branch_guard_realization_drift branch_guard_dangling_binding) do
186+ @code code
187+ test "#{@code} fails the realized_by-binding integrity check" do
188+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{@code => 1}))
189+
190+ binding_check =
191+ Enum.find(checks, fn c ->
192+ c.leg == "Spec → Code" and c.codes == ~w(branch_guard_realization_drift branch_guard_dangling_binding)
193+ end)
194+
195+ assert binding_check, "expected a Spec → Code row covering the realization-drift codes"
196+ assert binding_check.passed? == false
197+ end
198+ end
199+ end
200+
201+ describe "build_sync_checks — Spec → Tests triangle rows" do
202+ test "branch_guard_untested_realization fails the 'binding closure exercised' row" do
203+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{"branch_guard_untested_realization" => 1}))
204+
205+ row =
206+ Enum.find(checks, fn c ->
207+ c.leg == "Spec → Tests" and "branch_guard_untested_realization" in c.codes
208+ end)
209+
210+ assert row
211+ assert row.passed? == false
212+ end
213+
214+ for code <- ~w(requirement_without_test_tag branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag) do
215+ @code code
216+ test "#{@code} fails the 'must under tagged_tests has @tag spec:' row" do
217+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{@code => 1}))
218+
219+ row =
220+ Enum.find(checks, fn c ->
221+ c.leg == "Spec → Tests" and @code in c.codes
222+ end)
223+
224+ assert row
225+ assert row.passed? == false
226+ end
227+ end
228+ end
229+
230+ describe "build_sync_checks — Code → Tests triangle row" do
231+ for code <- ~w(branch_guard_untethered_test branch_guard_underspecified_realization) do
232+ @code code
233+ test "#{@code} fails the 'tagged tests exercise the subject they claim' row" do
234+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{@code => 1}))
235+
236+ row =
237+ Enum.find(checks, fn c ->
238+ c.leg == "Code → Tests" and @code in c.codes
239+ end)
240+
241+ assert row, "expected a Code → Tests row covering #{@code}"
242+ assert row.passed? == false
243+ end
244+ end
245+ end
246+
247+ describe "render_findings_list — leg + severity grouping" do
248+ # The global findings list groups findings by triangle leg first, then
249+ # by severity within each leg, with counts in each heading. The leg
250+ # mapping reuses build_sync_checks (the sync checklist's source of
251+ # truth) rather than maintaining a parallel table.
252+
253+ defp leg_finding(code, severity, opts \\ []) do
254+ %{
255+ "code" => code,
256+ "severity" => severity,
257+ "subject_id" => Keyword.get(opts, :subject_id),
258+ "message" => Keyword.get(opts, :message, "msg for #{code}")
259+ }
260+ end
261+
262+ test "an empty list renders nothing" do
263+ assert Html.render_findings_list([]) == ""
264+ end
265+
266+ test "leg_for_finding_code reuses the sync-checklist code-to-leg mapping" do
267+ # Each leg bucket has at least one canonical code that the existing
268+ # sync checklist (build_sync_checks) already classifies. If those
269+ # mappings ever drift, this test pins the contract.
270+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("overlap/duplicate_covers") == "Spec well-formed"
271+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("branch_guard_realization_drift") == "Spec ↔ Code"
272+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("requirement_without_test_tag") == "Spec ↔ Tests"
273+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("branch_guard_untethered_test") == "Code ↔ Tests"
274+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("verification_strength_below_minimum") == "Coverage"
275+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("append_only/requirement_deleted") == "Decisions"
276+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("branch_guard_unmapped_change") == "Branch"
277+ end
278+
279+ test "code_to_leg_lookup is sourced from build_sync_checks (no duplication)" do
280+ # Walk the sync checklist directly and confirm every code it knows
281+ # appears in the global-list lookup, mapped to a known bucket. This
282+ # is the audit knob that prevents accidental drift between the two.
283+ zeroed = %{
284+ findings_by_code: %{},
285+ affected_subject_count: 0,
286+ binding_count: 0,
287+ requirement_count: 0,
288+ verification_count: 0,
289+ adr_ref_count: 0,
290+ strength_breakdown: %{}
291+ }
292+
293+ lookup = Html.code_to_leg_lookup()
294+
295+ buckets = [
296+ "Spec well-formed",
297+ "Spec ↔ Code",
298+ "Spec ↔ Tests",
299+ "Code ↔ Tests",
300+ "Coverage",
301+ "Decisions",
302+ "Branch"
303+ ]
304+
305+ for check <- Html.build_sync_checks(zeroed),
306+ code <- check.codes do
307+ assert Map.has_key?(lookup, code),
308+ "code #{code} from sync-check leg #{inspect(check.leg)} is not in code_to_leg_lookup"
309+
310+ assert Map.get(lookup, code) in buckets,
311+ "code #{code} mapped to unexpected bucket #{inspect(Map.get(lookup, code))}"
312+ end
313+ end
314+
315+ test "an unknown code falls into the 'Other' bucket" do
316+ assert Html.leg_for_finding_code("totally_made_up_code") == "Other"
317+ end
318+
319+ test "groups appear in the canonical leg order" do
320+ findings = [
321+ leg_finding("branch_guard_unmapped_change", "warning"),
322+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error"),
323+ leg_finding("overlap/duplicate_covers", "error"),
324+ leg_finding("requirement_without_test_tag", "warning")
325+ ]
326+
327+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
328+
329+ # Index of each leg heading in the rendered HTML — must follow the
330+ # canonical order: Spec well-formed, Spec ↔ Code, Spec ↔ Tests, ...
331+ indices =
332+ for leg <- ["Spec well-formed", "Spec ↔ Code", "Spec ↔ Tests", "Branch"] do
333+ {leg, :binary.match(html, leg)}
334+ end
335+
336+ Enum.each(indices, fn {leg, m} ->
337+ refute m == :nomatch, "expected leg #{inspect(leg)} to render"
338+ end)
339+
340+ offsets = Enum.map(indices, fn {_, {pos, _}} -> pos end)
341+ assert offsets == Enum.sort(offsets), "groups did not render in canonical order"
342+ end
343+
344+ test "each group heading shows total count and per-severity counts" do
345+ findings = [
346+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error"),
347+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error"),
348+ leg_finding("branch_guard_dangling_binding", "warning")
349+ ]
350+
351+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
352+
353+ assert html =~ ~s|<h3 class="findings-group-heading">|
354+ assert html =~ ~s|<span class="findings-group-leg">Spec ↔ Code</span>|
355+ assert html =~ "3 findings"
356+ assert html =~ ~s|findings-group-sev-error|
357+ assert html =~ "2 error"
358+ assert html =~ ~s|findings-group-sev-warning|
359+ assert html =~ "1 warning"
360+ end
361+
362+ test "within a group, errors render before warnings before info" do
363+ findings = [
364+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "info", message: "info-finding"),
365+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "warning", message: "warn-finding"),
366+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error", message: "err-finding")
367+ ]
368+
369+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
370+
371+ err_pos = :binary.match(html, "err-finding") |> elem(0)
372+ warn_pos = :binary.match(html, "warn-finding") |> elem(0)
373+ info_pos = :binary.match(html, "info-finding") |> elem(0)
374+
375+ assert err_pos < warn_pos
376+ assert warn_pos < info_pos
377+ end
378+
379+ test "each finding still uses the tooltip(:severity, sev) mechanism" do
380+ findings = [leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error")]
381+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
382+
383+ # The severity span must carry the tooltip text from
384+ # tooltip(:severity, "error") — same mechanism the original list used.
385+ assert html =~ "Error — blocks the gate"
386+ end
387+
388+ test "the heading severity chip also carries the severity tooltip" do
389+ findings = [leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "warning")]
390+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
391+
392+ # The "1 warning" chip in the group heading must carry the warning
393+ # tooltip — the ticket calls out reusing tooltip(:severity, sev).
394+ assert html =~ ~s|findings-group-sev-warning|
395+ assert html =~ "Warning"
396+ end
397+
398+ test "an unclassifiable code lands in the 'Other' bucket at the end" do
399+ findings = [
400+ leg_finding("not_a_known_code", "warning"),
401+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error")
402+ ]
403+
404+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
405+
406+ spec_code_pos = :binary.match(html, "Spec ↔ Code") |> elem(0)
407+ other_pos = :binary.match(html, "Other") |> elem(0)
408+
409+ assert spec_code_pos < other_pos
410+ end
411+
412+ test "the outer summary shows the total finding count" do
413+ findings = [
414+ leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error"),
415+ leg_finding("branch_guard_unmapped_change", "warning"),
416+ leg_finding("requirement_without_test_tag", "warning")
417+ ]
418+
419+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
420+
421+ assert html =~ "All findings (3)"
422+ end
423+
424+ test "groups with no findings do not render" do
425+ findings = [leg_finding("branch_guard_realization_drift", "error")]
426+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
427+
428+ # Only the Spec ↔ Code group should be present; no Coverage / Branch / etc.
429+ refute html =~ ~s|<span class="findings-group-leg">Coverage</span>|
430+ refute html =~ ~s|<span class="findings-group-leg">Branch</span>|
431+ refute html =~ ~s|<span class="findings-group-leg">Decisions</span>|
432+ end
433+ end
434+
435+ describe "leg coverage parity with concepts.md vocabulary" do
436+ # This is a meta-check: every triangle code documented in concepts.md
437+ # is wired to at least one diagram leg or checklist row. If the spec
438+ # gains a new code class, this test should fail until the classifier
439+ # is updated.
440+ @diagram_triangle_codes ~w(
441+ branch_guard_realization_drift
442+ branch_guard_dangling_binding
443+ branch_guard_untested_realization
444+ branch_guard_untethered_test
445+ branch_guard_underspecified_realization
446+ requirement_without_test_tag
447+ branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag
448+ )
449+
450+ # overlap/* are within-spec consistency findings. They surface in the
451+ # "Spec files are well-formed" checklist row but do not change the
452+ # triangle's diagram edges (they're upstream of the triangle itself).
453+ @checklist_only_codes ~w(overlap/duplicate_covers overlap/must_stem_collision)
454+
455+ test "every documented triangle code is covered by at least one checklist row" do
456+ checks = Html.build_sync_checks(triage(%{}))
457+ lookup = checks_by_codes(checks)
458+
459+ for code <- @diagram_triangle_codes ++ @checklist_only_codes do
460+ assert Map.has_key?(lookup, code),
461+ "triangle code #{code} is not wired into any sync-check row"
462+ end
463+ end
464+
465+ test "every triangle code that has a diagram-edge mapping flips an edge to fail" do
466+ for code <- @diagram_triangle_codes do
467+ labels_fail =
468+ IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(triage(%{code => 1})))
469+ |> extract_labels("fail")
470+
471+ assert labels_fail != [],
472+ "triangle code #{code} did not flip any diagram edge to fail"
473+ end
474+ end
475+
476+ test "checklist-only codes do not flip diagram edges (they're upstream of the triangle)" do
477+ for code <- @checklist_only_codes do
478+ labels_fail =
479+ IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(triage(%{code => 1})))
480+ |> extract_labels("fail")
481+
482+ assert labels_fail == [],
483+ "checklist-only code #{code} unexpectedly flipped a diagram edge"
484+ end
485+ end
486+ end
487+
488+ describe "render_sync_diagram leg classifier — :degraded for detector_unavailable" do
489+ # covers: specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state
490+ test "no_coverage_artifact flips the evidence edge to degraded" do
491+ degraded =
492+ triage(%{}, %{
493+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{tests_to_coverage: %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}}
494+ })
495+
496+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(degraded))
497+
498+ assert "evidence" in extract_labels(html, "degraded"),
499+ "no_coverage_artifact should flip the evidence edge to :degraded"
500+
501+ refute "evidence" in extract_labels(html, "ok")
502+ refute "evidence" in extract_labels(html, "fail")
503+ end
504+
505+ test "debug_info_stripped flips the realized_by edge to degraded" do
506+ degraded =
507+ triage(%{}, %{
508+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{spec_to_code: %{"debug_info_stripped" => 1}}
509+ })
510+
511+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(degraded))
512+
513+ assert "realized_by" in extract_labels(html, "degraded")
514+ refute "realized_by" in extract_labels(html, "ok")
515+ end
516+
517+ test "umbrella_unsupported flips the realized_by edge to degraded" do
518+ degraded =
519+ triage(%{}, %{
520+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{spec_to_code: %{"umbrella_unsupported" => 1}}
521+ })
522+
523+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(degraded))
524+
525+ assert "realized_by" in extract_labels(html, "degraded")
526+ end
527+
528+ test "a fail finding on the same leg supersedes a degraded reason" do
529+ both =
530+ triage(%{"branch_guard_realization_drift" => 1}, %{
531+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{spec_to_code: %{"debug_info_stripped" => 1}}
532+ })
533+
534+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(both))
535+
536+ assert "realized_by" in extract_labels(html, "fail"),
537+ "a real failure must outrank a detector_unavailable on the same leg"
538+
539+ refute "realized_by" in extract_labels(html, "degraded")
540+ end
541+
542+ test "the degraded edge renders with a ? glyph (not ✓ or ✗)" do
543+ degraded =
544+ triage(%{}, %{
545+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{tests_to_coverage: %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}}
546+ })
547+
548+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(degraded))
549+
550+ assert html =~ ~s|sync-edge sync-edge-degraded|
551+
552+ # Pull the icon span out of the degraded edge specifically.
553+ assert Regex.match?(
554+ ~r|sync-edge sync-edge-degraded.*?sync-edge-icon">\?</span>|s,
555+ html
556+ ),
557+ "the degraded edge should render its glyph as ? (not ✓ or ✗)"
558+ end
559+
560+ test "the degraded edge tooltip names every distinct reason on that leg" do
561+ degraded =
562+ triage(%{}, %{
563+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{
564+ spec_to_code: %{"debug_info_stripped" => 2, "umbrella_unsupported" => 1}
565+ }
566+ })
567+
568+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_sync_diagram(degraded))
569+
570+ [tooltip] =
571+ Regex.run(
572+ ~r|sync-edge sync-edge-degraded[^>]*title="([^"]+)"|,
573+ html,
574+ capture: :all_but_first
575+ )
576+
577+ assert tooltip =~ "debug_info_stripped"
578+ assert tooltip =~ "umbrella_unsupported"
579+ end
580+ end
581+
582+ describe "render_degraded_banner" do
583+ # covers: specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state
584+ test "renders a banner when any leg carries a detector_unavailable finding" do
585+ banner =
586+ Html.render_degraded_banner(
587+ triage(%{}, %{
588+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{
589+ tests_to_coverage: %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}
590+ }
591+ })
592+ )
593+
594+ assert banner =~ ~s|class="sync-degraded-banner"|
595+ assert banner =~ "no_coverage_artifact"
596+ assert banner =~ "Partial report"
597+ end
598+
599+ test "renders an empty string when no detector_unavailable findings exist" do
600+ banner = Html.render_degraded_banner(triage(%{}))
601+
602+ assert banner == ""
603+ end
604+
605+ test "the banner enumerates every distinct reason across legs" do
606+ banner =
607+ Html.render_degraded_banner(
608+ triage(%{}, %{
609+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{
610+ spec_to_code: %{"debug_info_stripped" => 2, "umbrella_unsupported" => 1},
611+ tests_to_coverage: %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}
612+ }
613+ })
614+ )
615+
616+ assert banner =~ "debug_info_stripped"
617+ assert banner =~ "umbrella_unsupported"
618+ assert banner =~ "no_coverage_artifact"
619+ end
620+ end
621+
622+ describe "build_sync_checks — degraded rows" do
623+ # covers: specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state
624+ test "a tests_to_coverage detector_unavailable marks tests/coverage rows as degraded" do
625+ checks =
626+ Html.build_sync_checks(
627+ triage(%{}, %{
628+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{tests_to_coverage: %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}}
629+ })
630+ )
631+
632+ coverage_check = Enum.find(checks, &(&1.leg == "Coverage"))
633+
634+ assert coverage_check
635+ assert coverage_check.passed? == true
636+ assert Map.get(coverage_check, :degraded?) == true
637+ assert Map.get(coverage_check, :degraded_reasons) == %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}
638+ end
639+
640+ test "rows on legs with no detector_unavailable are not marked degraded" do
641+ checks =
642+ Html.build_sync_checks(
643+ triage(%{}, %{
644+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{tests_to_coverage: %{"no_coverage_artifact" => 1}}
645+ })
646+ )
647+
648+ spec_well_formed = Enum.find(checks, &(&1.leg == "Spec"))
649+
650+ assert spec_well_formed
651+ refute Map.get(spec_well_formed, :degraded?, false)
652+ end
653+
654+ test "a failing finding on a degraded leg keeps the row failing, not degraded" do
655+ checks =
656+ Html.build_sync_checks(
657+ triage(%{"branch_guard_realization_drift" => 1}, %{
658+ detector_unavailable_by_leg: %{spec_to_code: %{"debug_info_stripped" => 1}}
659+ })
660+ )
661+
662+ drift_row =
663+ Enum.find(checks, fn c ->
664+ "branch_guard_realization_drift" in c.codes
665+ end)
666+
667+ assert drift_row
668+ assert drift_row.passed? == false
669+ # degraded? requires passed? — when a real failure exists, the row
670+ # is fail, not degraded.
671+ refute Map.get(drift_row, :degraded?, false)
672+ end
673+ end
674+
675+ # covers: specled.spec_review.decisions_governance_inline
676+ describe "render_decisions_changed — append_only governance findings" do
677+ defp finding(code, opts \\ []) do
678+ %{
679+ "code" => code,
680+ "severity" => Keyword.get(opts, :severity, "error"),
681+ "subject_id" => Keyword.get(opts, :subject_id, "x"),
682+ "entity_id" => Keyword.get(opts, :entity_id, "x.req_a"),
683+ "message" =>
684+ Keyword.get(
685+ opts,
686+ :message,
687+ """
688+ Requirement `x.req_a` was present at base and is absent at head.
689+
690+ ```
691+ fix: author an ADR with change_type in {deprecates, weakens, narrows-scope, adds-exception} and affects: [x.req_a] — or restore the requirement in its spec file.
692+ ```
693+ """
694+ )
695+ }
696+ end
697+
698+ test "with no decisions and no findings, the panel still renders the empty message" do
699+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_decisions_changed([], %{}, []))
700+
701+ assert html =~ "Decisions changed (0)"
702+ assert html =~ "No ADR files changed"
703+ refute html =~ "Governance violations"
704+ end
705+
706+ test "an unauthorized requirement deletion surfaces in a Governance violations subsection even when no ADR file changed" do
707+ html =
708+ IO.iodata_to_binary(
709+ Html.render_decisions_changed([], %{}, [finding("append_only/requirement_deleted")])
710+ )
711+
712+ assert html =~ "Governance violations"
713+ assert html =~ "append_only/requirement_deleted"
714+
715+ assert html =~
716+ "Requirement `x.req_a` was present at base and is absent at head"
717+
718+ assert html =~ "governance-finding-fix"
719+
720+ assert html =~
721+ "fix: author an ADR with change_type in {deprecates, weakens, narrows-scope, adds-exception}"
722+ end
723+
724+ test "the rendered finding preserves the code-fenced fix block emitted by AppendOnly.analyze" do
725+ html =
726+ IO.iodata_to_binary(
727+ Html.render_decisions_changed([], %{}, [finding("append_only/requirement_deleted")])
728+ )
729+
730+ # The fix block lives inside <pre class="governance-finding-fix"><code>...</code></pre>
731+ # and is the verbatim text that the finding generator wrote.
732+ assert html =~ ~r|<pre class="governance-finding-fix"><code>fix:[^<]+</code></pre>|s
733+ end
734+
735+ test "an append_only/* finding whose entity_id matches a present ADR renders inline under that ADR card" do
736+ decision = %{
737+ id: "specled.decision.adr_x",
738+ file: ".spec/decisions/adr_x.md",
739+ status: "accepted",
740+ change_type: "weakens",
741+ affects: ["x.req_a"]
742+ }
743+
744+ adr = %{
745+ id: "specled.decision.adr_x",
746+ file: ".spec/decisions/adr_x.md",
747+ title: "ADR X",
748+ status: "accepted",
749+ date: nil,
750+ change_type: "weakens",
751+ affects: ["x.req_a"],
752+ body_text: "body",
753+ change_status: :modified
754+ }
755+
756+ finding =
757+ finding("append_only/adr_affects_widened",
758+ entity_id: "specled.decision.adr_x",
759+ message:
760+ "ADR `specled.decision.adr_x` was status: accepted at base but its structural fields changed at head.\n\n```\nfix: revert the field edit on the accepted ADR.\n```\n"
761+ )
762+
763+ html =
764+ IO.iodata_to_binary(
765+ Html.render_decisions_changed(
766+ [decision],
767+ %{"specled.decision.adr_x" => adr},
768+ [finding]
769+ )
770+ )
771+
772+ assert html =~ "Decisions changed (1)"
773+ assert html =~ "Governance findings naming this ADR"
774+ assert html =~ "append_only/adr_affects_widened"
775+ # Inline rendering means the orphan subsection is NOT used for this one
776+ refute html =~ "Governance violations (1)"
777+ end
778+
779+ test "mixed: an inline-mapped finding and an orphan finding render in their respective places" do
780+ decision = %{
781+ id: "specled.decision.adr_x",
782+ file: ".spec/decisions/adr_x.md",
783+ status: "accepted",
784+ change_type: "weakens",
785+ affects: ["x.req_a"]
786+ }
787+
788+ adr = %{
789+ id: "specled.decision.adr_x",
790+ file: ".spec/decisions/adr_x.md",
791+ title: "ADR X",
792+ status: "accepted",
793+ date: nil,
794+ change_type: "weakens",
795+ affects: ["x.req_a"],
796+ body_text: "body",
797+ change_status: :modified
798+ }
799+
800+ inline =
801+ finding("append_only/adr_affects_widened",
802+ entity_id: "specled.decision.adr_x",
803+ message: "ADR widened.\n\n```\nfix: revert.\n```\n"
804+ )
805+
806+ orphan = finding("append_only/requirement_deleted")
807+
808+ html =
809+ IO.iodata_to_binary(
810+ Html.render_decisions_changed(
811+ [decision],
812+ %{"specled.decision.adr_x" => adr},
813+ [inline, orphan]
814+ )
815+ )
816+
817+ # Orphan in the top subsection
818+ assert html =~ "Governance violations (1)"
819+ assert html =~ "append_only/requirement_deleted"
820+ # Inline under the ADR card
821+ assert html =~ "Governance findings naming this ADR"
822+ assert html =~ "append_only/adr_affects_widened"
823+ end
824+
825+ test "non-append_only findings are ignored by the Decisions panel" do
826+ noise = %{
827+ "code" => "branch_guard_realization_drift",
828+ "severity" => "error",
829+ "subject_id" => "x",
830+ "entity_id" => "x.req_a",
831+ "message" => "drift"
832+ }
833+
834+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_decisions_changed([], %{}, [noise]))
835+
836+ assert html =~ "Decisions changed (0)"
837+ refute html =~ "Governance violations"
838+ refute html =~ "branch_guard_realization_drift"
839+ end
840+ end
841+
842+ defp render_findings(findings), do: IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_findings_list(findings))
843+
844+ defp untethered_finding(claimed, observed_owners, opts \\ []) do
845+ %{
846+ "code" => "branch_guard_untethered_test",
847+ "severity" => Keyword.get(opts, :severity, "info"),
848+ "subject_id" => claimed,
849+ "observed_owners" => observed_owners,
850+ "message" =>
851+ Keyword.get(opts, :message, "test claims #{claimed} but hits #{Enum.join(observed_owners, ", ")}")
852+ }
853+ end
854+
855+ describe "render_findings_list — untethered_test claims/hits link" do
856+ # A branch_guard_untethered_test finding's `subject_id` is the *claimed*
857+ # subject (the one the test's `@tag spec:` named) while the test's
858+ # coverage actually exercises a different subject — recorded in
859+ # `observed_owners`. The reviewer needs both anchors so the misalignment
860+ # IS the headline; linking only to the claimed subject sends them to a
861+ # card with no evidence.
862+
863+ test "renders 'claims A, hits B' with anchors to both subjects when observed_owners present" do
864+ html = render_findings([untethered_finding("subject.a", ["subject.b"], message: "...")])
865+
866+ assert html =~ "finding-subject-pair"
867+ assert html =~ ~s|<span class="finding-subject-label">claims</span>|
868+ assert html =~ ~s|<span class="finding-subject-label">, hits</span>|
869+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-a"|
870+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-b"|
871+ assert html =~ ">subject.a<"
872+ assert html =~ ">subject.b<"
873+ end
874+
875+ test "preserves order: claimed appears before observed in the rendered string" do
876+ html = render_findings([untethered_finding("subject.a", ["subject.b"])])
877+
878+ claimed_pos = :binary.match(html, "subject-subject-a") |> elem(0)
879+ observed_pos = :binary.match(html, "subject-subject-b") |> elem(0)
880+
881+ assert claimed_pos < observed_pos,
882+ "claimed subject should be rendered before observed owner"
883+ end
884+
885+ test "renders multiple observed owners as comma-separated anchors" do
886+ html = render_findings([untethered_finding("subject.a", ["subject.b", "subject.c"])])
887+
888+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-a"|
889+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-b"|
890+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-c"|
891+ end
892+
893+ test "falls back to single claimed link when observed_owners is missing" do
894+ finding = %{
895+ "code" => "branch_guard_untethered_test",
896+ "severity" => "info",
897+ "subject_id" => "subject.a",
898+ "message" => "..."
899+ }
900+
901+ html = render_findings([finding])
902+
903+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-a"|
904+ refute html =~ "finding-subject-pair"
905+ refute html =~ "finding-subject-label"
906+ end
907+
908+ test "falls back to single claimed link when observed_owners is empty list" do
909+ html = render_findings([untethered_finding("subject.a", [], message: "...")])
910+
911+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-a"|
912+ refute html =~ "finding-subject-pair"
913+ refute html =~ "finding-subject-label"
914+ end
915+
916+ test "non-untethered findings still render a single subject link" do
917+ finding = %{
918+ "code" => "branch_guard_untested_realization",
919+ "severity" => "warning",
920+ "subject_id" => "subject.a",
921+ "observed_owners" => ["subject.b"],
922+ "message" => "..."
923+ }
924+
925+ html = render_findings([finding])
926+
927+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-a"|
928+ refute html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-b"|
929+ refute html =~ "finding-subject-pair"
930+ refute html =~ "finding-subject-label"
931+ end
932+
933+ test "untethered finding without subject_id still renders observed owners" do
934+ finding = %{
935+ "code" => "branch_guard_untethered_test",
936+ "severity" => "info",
937+ "subject_id" => nil,
938+ "observed_owners" => ["subject.b"],
939+ "message" => "..."
940+ }
941+
942+ html = render_findings([finding])
943+
944+ assert html =~ ~s|href="#subject-subject-b"|
945+ end
946+ end
947+
948+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
949+ describe "render_coverage_tab — per-requirement bind-closure view" do
950+ defp coverage_subject(opts) do
951+ %{
952+ id: Keyword.get(opts, :id, "subj.a"),
953+ bindings: Keyword.get(opts, :bindings, %{}),
954+ requirements:
955+ Keyword.get(opts, :requirements, [
956+ %{
957+ "id" => "subj.a.req1",
958+ "statement" => "First requirement statement.",
959+ "priority" => "must"
960+ },
961+ %{
962+ "id" => "subj.a.req2",
963+ "statement" => "Second requirement statement.",
964+ "priority" => "must"
965+ }
966+ ]),
967+ claims_by_req: Keyword.get(opts, :claims_by_req, %{}),
968+ closure_reach: Keyword.get(opts, :closure_reach, %{status: :ok, by_requirement: %{}})
969+ }
970+ end
971+
972+ test "renders \"Closure: N MFAs. Reached: M (by tests …). Unreached: K.\" per requirement" do
973+ reach = %{
974+ status: :ok,
975+ by_requirement: %{
976+ "subj.a.req1" => %{
977+ closure_mfa_count: 4,
978+ closure_file_count: 2,
979+ reached_files: ["lib/a.ex"],
980+ unreached_files: ["lib/a_extra.ex"],
981+ reaching_tests: ["test/a_test.exs :: alpha", "test/b_test.exs :: beta"]
982+ },
983+ "subj.a.req2" => %{
984+ closure_mfa_count: 1,
985+ closure_file_count: 1,
986+ reached_files: [],
987+ unreached_files: ["lib/x.ex"],
988+ reaching_tests: []
989+ }
990+ }
991+ }
992+
993+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_coverage_tab(coverage_subject(closure_reach: reach)))
994+
995+ # Both requirement rows render their closure summary line.
996+ assert html =~ "Closure:</span> 4 MFAs."
997+ assert html =~ "Reached: 1"
998+ assert html =~ "test/a_test.exs :: alpha"
999+ assert html =~ "test/b_test.exs :: beta"
1000+ assert html =~ "Unreached: 1."
1001+
1002+ # The unreached requirement renders Reached: 0 and no test list.
1003+ assert html =~ "Closure:</span> 1 MFA."
1004+ end
1005+
1006+ test "omits \"by tests …\" when no tests reach the requirement's closure" do
1007+ reach = %{
1008+ status: :ok,
1009+ by_requirement: %{
1010+ "subj.a.req1" => %{
1011+ closure_mfa_count: 2,
1012+ closure_file_count: 1,
1013+ reached_files: [],
1014+ unreached_files: ["lib/a.ex"],
1015+ reaching_tests: []
1016+ }
1017+ }
1018+ }
1019+
1020+ html =
1021+ IO.iodata_to_binary(
1022+ Html.render_coverage_tab(
1023+ coverage_subject(
1024+ closure_reach: reach,
1025+ requirements: [
1026+ %{"id" => "subj.a.req1", "statement" => "S", "priority" => "must"}
1027+ ]
1028+ )
1029+ )
1030+ )
1031+
1032+ assert html =~ "Reached: 0."
1033+ refute html =~ "by tests"
1034+ end
1035+
1036+ test "renders the empty-closure form when the requirement has no closure MFAs" do
1037+ reach = %{
1038+ status: :ok,
1039+ by_requirement: %{
1040+ "subj.a.req1" => %{
1041+ closure_mfa_count: 0,
1042+ closure_file_count: 0,
1043+ reached_files: [],
1044+ unreached_files: [],
1045+ reaching_tests: []
1046+ }
1047+ }
1048+ }
1049+
1050+ html =
1051+ IO.iodata_to_binary(
1052+ Html.render_coverage_tab(
1053+ coverage_subject(
1054+ closure_reach: reach,
1055+ requirements: [
1056+ %{"id" => "subj.a.req1", "statement" => "S", "priority" => "must"}
1057+ ]
1058+ )
1059+ )
1060+ )
1061+
1062+ assert html =~ ~s|class="cov-closure" data-empty="true"|
1063+ assert html =~ "Closure:</span> 0 MFAs."
1064+ end
1065+
1066+ test "renders the \"coverage artifact unavailable\" banner when status is :no_coverage_artifact" do
1067+ reach = %{status: :no_coverage_artifact, by_requirement: %{}}
1068+
1069+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_coverage_tab(coverage_subject(closure_reach: reach)))
1070+
1071+ assert html =~ "Coverage artifact unavailable"
1072+ assert html =~ "mix spec.cover.test"
1073+ # Per-row closure lines are suppressed when the artifact is missing —
1074+ # the page-level degraded banner already advertises the situation.
1075+ refute html =~ ~s|class="cov-closure"|
1076+ end
1077+
1078+ test "renders the \"binding closure unavailable\" banner when status is :no_tracer_manifest" do
1079+ reach = %{status: :no_tracer_manifest, by_requirement: %{}}
1080+
1081+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_coverage_tab(coverage_subject(closure_reach: reach)))
1082+
1083+ assert html =~ "Binding closure unavailable"
1084+ assert html =~ "tracer manifest"
1085+ refute html =~ ~s|class="cov-closure"|
1086+ end
1087+
1088+ test "is backwards-compatible when the subject view-model has no closure_reach key" do
1089+ subject = %{
1090+ id: "subj.a",
1091+ bindings: %{},
1092+ requirements: [
1093+ %{"id" => "subj.a.req1", "statement" => "S", "priority" => "must"}
1094+ ],
1095+ claims_by_req: %{}
1096+ }
1097+
1098+ # No raise; the requirement renders without a closure summary.
1099+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_coverage_tab(subject))
1100+
1101+ assert html =~ "subj.a.req1"
1102+ refute html =~ ~s|class="cov-closure"|
1103+ refute html =~ "Coverage artifact unavailable"
1104+ end
1105+ end
1106+end
test/specled_ex/review_test.exs
@@ -106,6 +106,300 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.ReviewTest do
106106 end
107107 end
108108
109+ describe "Html.render_subject_binding_health/2" do
110+ alias SpecLedEx.Review.Html
111+
112+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
113+ test "renders the no-realized_by chip when bindings is empty" do
114+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(%{}, []))
115+
116+ assert html =~ "0 bindings (no realized_by declared)"
117+ assert html =~ "badge-binding-empty"
118+ refute html =~ "all valid"
119+ refute html =~ "dangling"
120+ end
121+
122+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
123+ test "renders the no-realized_by chip when bindings is nil" do
124+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(nil, []))
125+
126+ assert html =~ "0 bindings (no realized_by declared)"
127+ assert html =~ "badge-binding-empty"
128+ end
129+
130+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
131+ test "renders the all-valid chip when bindings exist and no dangling findings are present" do
132+ bindings = %{
133+ "api_boundary" => ["MyApp.API.do_thing/1"],
134+ "implementation" => ["MyApp.Impl"]
135+ }
136+
137+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(bindings, []))
138+
139+ assert html =~ "2 bindings, all valid"
140+ assert html =~ "badge-binding-valid"
141+ refute html =~ "dangling"
142+ end
143+
144+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
145+ test "uses singular 'binding' when there is exactly one" do
146+ bindings = %{"api_boundary" => ["MyApp.API.do_thing/1"]}
147+
148+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(bindings, []))
149+
150+ assert html =~ "1 binding, all valid"
151+ refute html =~ "1 bindings"
152+ end
153+
154+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
155+ test "renders the dangling chip with the dangling count when dangling-binding findings exist" do
156+ bindings = %{
157+ "api_boundary" => ["MyApp.API.do_thing/1", "MyApp.API.other/0"],
158+ "implementation" => ["MyApp.Impl"]
159+ }
160+
161+ findings = [
162+ %{"code" => "branch_guard_dangling_binding", "severity" => "error"},
163+ %{"code" => "branch_guard_dangling_binding", "severity" => "error"},
164+ %{"code" => "some_other_finding", "severity" => "warning"}
165+ ]
166+
167+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(bindings, findings))
168+
169+ assert html =~ "3 bindings, 2 dangling"
170+ assert html =~ "badge-binding-dangling"
171+ refute html =~ "all valid"
172+ end
173+
174+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
175+ test "ignores findings whose code is not branch_guard_dangling_binding" do
176+ bindings = %{"api_boundary" => ["MyApp.API.do_thing/1"]}
177+
178+ findings = [
179+ %{"code" => "branch_guard_realization_drift", "severity" => "error"},
180+ %{"code" => "overlap_subject", "severity" => "warning"}
181+ ]
182+
183+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(bindings, findings))
184+
185+ assert html =~ "1 binding, all valid"
186+ refute html =~ "dangling"
187+ end
188+
189+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
190+ test "treats a single non-list MFA value as one binding (List.wrap parity with bindings dl)" do
191+ bindings = %{"implementation" => "MyApp.Impl"}
192+
193+ html = IO.iodata_to_binary(Html.render_subject_binding_health(bindings, []))
194+
195+ assert html =~ "1 binding, all valid"
196+ end
197+ end
198+
199+ describe "subject card binding-health badge integration" do
200+ alias SpecLedEx.Review.Html
201+
202+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.inline_finding_badges"
203+ test "rendered HTML carries a binding-health badge on each subject card", %{root: root} do
204+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
205+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
206+
207+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
208+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
209+ html = view |> Html.render() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
210+
211+ assert html =~ "badge-binding-health"
212+ # The seed subject declares no realized_by, so it should land in the
213+ # "no realized_by declared" state.
214+ assert html =~ "0 bindings (no realized_by declared)"
215+ end
216+ end
217+
218+ # covers: specled.spec_review.coverage_tab_bind_closure
219+ describe "Coverage tab bind-closure integration" do
220+ alias SpecLedEx.Review.Html
221+
222+ test "build_view attaches closure_reach to each affected subject", %{root: root} do
223+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
224+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
225+
226+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
227+
228+ view =
229+ Review.build_view(index, root,
230+ base: "main",
231+ closure_reach_opts: [
232+ tracer_edges: %{},
233+ coverage_records: :no_coverage_artifact
234+ ]
235+ )
236+
237+ assert [subject] = view.affected_subjects
238+ assert is_map(subject.closure_reach)
239+ assert Map.has_key?(subject.closure_reach, :status)
240+ assert Map.has_key?(subject.closure_reach, :by_requirement)
241+ end
242+
243+ test "rendered Coverage tab degrades to a 'coverage artifact unavailable' banner when records are missing",
244+ %{root: root} do
245+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
246+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
247+
248+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
249+
250+ view =
251+ Review.build_view(index, root,
252+ base: "main",
253+ closure_reach_opts: [
254+ # An empty edges map degrades to :no_tracer_manifest first; pass
255+ # a non-empty map so the coverage status path is exercised.
256+ tracer_edges: %{{Auth, :login, 0} => []},
257+ coverage_records: :no_coverage_artifact
258+ ]
259+ )
260+
261+ html = view |> Html.render() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
262+
263+ assert html =~ "Coverage artifact unavailable"
264+ end
265+
266+ test "rendered Coverage tab degrades to a 'binding closure unavailable' banner when tracer manifest is missing",
267+ %{root: root} do
268+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
269+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
270+
271+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
272+
273+ view =
274+ Review.build_view(index, root,
275+ base: "main",
276+ closure_reach_opts: [
277+ tracer_edges: %{},
278+ coverage_records: []
279+ ]
280+ )
281+
282+ html = view |> Html.render() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
283+
284+ assert html =~ "Binding closure unavailable"
285+ end
286+ end
287+
288+ describe "no_realized_by detector_unavailable synthesis" do
289+ alias SpecLedEx.Review.Html
290+
291+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code"
292+ test "synthesizes a detector_unavailable finding for affected subjects with no realized_by",
293+ %{root: root} do
294+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
295+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
296+
297+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
298+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
299+
300+ assert [synth] =
301+ view.all_findings
302+ |> Enum.filter(fn f ->
303+ f["code"] == "detector_unavailable" and f["reason"] == "no_realized_by"
304+ end)
305+
306+ assert synth["subject_id"] == "auth.subject"
307+ assert synth["severity"] == "info"
308+
309+ assert synth["message"] =~ "no `realized_by`" or synth["message"] =~ "no realized_by"
310+
311+ # The triage's leg aggregation must place the synthesized reason on
312+ # SPEC ↔ CODE so the diagram surfaces the leg as :degraded.
313+ assert get_in(view.triage.detector_unavailable_by_leg, [:spec_to_code, "no_realized_by"]) ==
314+ 1
315+ end
316+
317+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code"
318+ test "does not synthesize a finding when the subject declares any non-empty realized_by tier",
319+ %{root: root} do
320+ setup_repo_with_realized_by(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.", %{
321+ "api_boundary" => ["MyApp.API.do_thing/1"]
322+ })
323+
324+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
325+
326+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
327+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
328+
329+ synth =
330+ Enum.filter(view.all_findings, fn f ->
331+ f["code"] == "detector_unavailable" and f["reason"] == "no_realized_by"
332+ end)
333+
334+ assert synth == []
335+
336+ assert get_in(view.triage.detector_unavailable_by_leg, [:spec_to_code, "no_realized_by"]) ==
337+ nil
338+ end
339+
340+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code"
341+ test "does not synthesize a finding when only a requirement declares realized_by",
342+ %{root: root} do
343+ init_git_repo(root)
344+
345+ write_subject_spec(
346+ root,
347+ "auth_subject",
348+ meta: %{
349+ "id" => "auth.subject",
350+ "kind" => "module",
351+ "status" => "active",
352+ "summary" => "Auth.",
353+ "surface" => ["lib/auth.ex"]
354+ },
355+ requirements: [
356+ %{
357+ "id" => "auth.subject.do_thing",
358+ "statement" => "Auth.do_thing/1 logs the caller.",
359+ "priority" => "must",
360+ "stability" => "evolving",
361+ "realized_by" => %{"api_boundary" => ["MyApp.API.do_thing/1"]}
362+ }
363+ ]
364+ )
365+
366+ write_files(root, %{"lib/auth.ex" => "defmodule Auth do\nend\n"})
367+ commit_all(root, "initial")
368+
369+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
370+
371+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
372+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
373+
374+ synth =
375+ Enum.filter(view.all_findings, fn f ->
376+ f["code"] == "detector_unavailable" and f["reason"] == "no_realized_by"
377+ end)
378+
379+ assert synth == [],
380+ "expected no synthesized finding when a requirement declares realized_by"
381+ end
382+
383+ @tag spec: "specled.spec_review.no_realized_by_degrades_spec_to_code"
384+ test "rendered HTML degrades the realized_by leg and surfaces the partial-report banner with the no_realized_by reason",
385+ %{root: root} do
386+ setup_repo(root, "auth_subject", "Auth.")
387+ change_subject_file(root, "auth_subject")
388+
389+ index = SpecLedEx.index(root)
390+ view = Review.build_view(index, root, base: "main")
391+ html = view |> Html.render() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
392+
393+ # Diagram leg is :degraded with a `?` glyph.
394+ assert html =~ "sync-edge sync-edge-degraded"
395+
396+ # Partial-report banner enumerates `no_realized_by` as a reason.
397+ assert html =~ "sync-degraded-banner"
398+ assert html =~ "no_realized_by"
399+ assert html =~ "Partial report"
400+ end
401+ end
402+
109403 # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
110404 # helpers
111405 # ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -129,6 +423,26 @@ defmodule SpecLedEx.ReviewTest do
129423 commit_all(root, "initial")
130424 end
131425
426+ defp setup_repo_with_realized_by(root, name, statement, realized_by) do
427+ init_git_repo(root)

444+ end
